songs.subject: (grok complex)
songs.lyrics:
Every morning I step out barefoot with that dented blue can
Jar of nutrients in my other hand, city still half-asleep downstairs
Basil's emerald, almost too bright, fern curls soft like it's shy
Tomato fuzzy, monstera just learning how to split its own disguise
I dip my finger in the dirt, some pots still damp from yesterday
Others dry and begging, so I mix the drops till the water turns gold
Like sunlight learned how to melt
I pour it slow, watch the soil drink, turn dark and soft and full
Roots down there stretching quiet, building stems and secret leaves
They reach up with every green hand, catch the sun the way I wish I could
You did all this, they seem to say
Yeah... so did you
Day after day I turn their pots so every side gets fair
Basil thickens up and smells like summer when I brush my sleeve
Fern spills over like a green waterfall nobody asked for but I love
Tomato climbs its crooked stake, throws yellow flowers at the bees
I pour it slow, watch the soil drink, turn dark and soft and full
Leaves unfurl bigger, glossier, monstera finally bold
They pull the light and water in, make their own food like it's nothing
You did all this, they seem to say
Yeah... so did you
By July the balcony's a jungle, birds land just to rest a minute
Air smells alive, like something breathing back at me
I tell 'em "Look what you became"
Wind moves through and they answer, real soft, like they mean it
I pour it slow... soil drinks... roots do their thing below
We built this tiny wild together, no one keeping score
They grew for me, I grew a little too, I guess
You did all this
Yeah...
So did you

Yeah...
So did you

songs.subject: (grok complex)
songs.lyrics:
In Aetheryn where isles drift slow,
Above the endless silver glow,
The Threadweavers pull the shining strands
That bind the sky and shape the lands.
Young Elowen, eyes like breaking dawn,
Saw the fray where others saw none,
The Loom was skipping, colors bled,
But elders said the threads were never dead.

Mend the threads, oh mend them true,
Don't cut or steal what isn't yours to take.
With open hands and heart laid bare,
Weave the world back from the break!
Silver lines that hold the night,
Elowen calls them back to light!

The Archweaver's robes turned dark as sin,
Corvin whispered, "Let the old world end.
I'll harvest magic, build anew,
While every isle falls into blue."
He tore the Loom with greedy hands,
Silver blood across the sands.
The floating stones began to spin-
But one small girl refused to give in.

She stood on nothing but her will,
Stitched the empty air until
The fraying edges burned like flame.
She reached inside his stolen name,
Pulled the poisoned thread of greed,
And every stolen spark was freed.
The Loom sang loud, the sky turned gold,
The broken world began to hold.

Now children wear the starlight robes,
They learn to listen where it throbs-
The hidden fray, the quiet tear,
The places only hope can mend with care.
No more pulling just to own,
No more breaking thrones of stone.
The First Mender walks the misty height,
And every thread remembers her light.

Mend the threads, oh mend them true,
Don't cut or steal what isn't yours to take.
With open hands and heart laid bare,
Weave the world back from the break!
Silver lines that hold the night,
Forever answer Elowen's light!

(End on a single sustained note fading into wind)

songs.subject: (grok complex)
songs.lyrics:
The block got its own weather, laugh one day, next day thunder
Sunshine from the corner store, then the air get tight like hunger
I thought the chains made me untouchable, heavy on the neck but light on the soul
Till I learned looking free ain't the same as being free - that's the cold truth I stole
Malik learned early, block had moods like a living thing, breathing
Kids racing bikes between parked cars, music bouncing off the brick, scheming
Older boys posted at Ray's Liquor, clean sneakers, heavy chains gleaming
Laughed like the world owed 'em, nobody talked down, nobody stepped on their dreams
One nod from Darnell and I felt seen for the first time, chest puffed
Home the fridge hummed louder than any conversation, Mom gone working nights, rough
Little brother outgrew his shoes again, bills on the table looking like locked doors, tough
So when he said "just watch the corner, few hours, easy cash," I told myself it wasn't much
For a minute it felt beautiful - crew called me family, bought food, gave rides
Taught me how to move like fear was luggage other niggas carried on they backs, no lie
Pockets got heavier, girls noticed, people stepped aside when I walked by
But the shine had cracks you couldn't see till the lights flipped
J-Rock - loudest joker on the block - crying behind the dumpster, fists to his eyes, sick
Darnell stopped sleeping, jumped at every siren like the reaper on his dick
Younger kids already learning the same hard faces, same tight lips
Money came fast but peace never knocked, just kept circling the block like it missed
The block got its own weather, laugh one day, next day thunder
Sunshine from the corner store, then the air get tight like hunger
I thought the chains made me untouchable, heavy on the neck but light on the soul
Till I learned looking free ain't the same as being free - that's the cold truth I stole
One night little brother looked up, eyes wide like worship, asked "You famous on the block now?"
Something cold cracked open in my chest, like the fridge finally shut down
Next morning I walked past Ray's without stopping, legs shaking, pride burning hot
Darnell called my name twice, voice cracking, but I kept walking, didn't look back, not
Bills still on the table, nothing magically changed, same locked doors, same grind
But for the first time I understood what the older boys never said out loud -
Looking untouchable ain't the same as being free... and that truth hit different this time
Block still got its weather... I just stopped letting it rain on me.

songs.subject: (grok complex)
songs.lyrics:
Mornings I fix kettles, afternoons I lose
The broom says "I'm retired" and I say "me too"
Used to teach ravens tax law just to watch kings squirm
Now the only spell I remember is how to make coffee burn
Girl shows up shaking, glass jar under her arm
Blue flame inside crying like it's done with harm
She says "It fell from the moon-can you put it back?"
I almost laugh. I almost tell her I can't
But I say go home, little spell, find your place in the dark
Warm on her cheek for a heartbeat, then you spark
Stitch one bright thread through that torn-up sky
I ain't great anymore... but damn I'm useful tonight
Wind-stairs wobbled under us, paper gulls flew by
She never once asked if I still knew how to fly
I raised the staff anyway, felt stupid and small
Forgot the fancy language, just let the quiet fall
Flame didn't hiss, didn't curse, didn't try to impress
Just hovered soft and said goodbye the way real things do, I guess
She looked at me like maybe I was magic after all
I told her "No... but I can still fix what's broke, that's all"
Final Chorus (strip it back - voice + one guitar)
Go home, little spell... you don't owe me the show
Warm on her cheek then you rise on your own
I may not summon storms or bend the mirrors right
But I sent one lost thing home and the night feels bright
Tag (almost spoken)
Stronger magic than I ever earned... turns out.

songs.subject: (grok complex)
songs.lyrics:
Pale sky, loud street, same guarded walk I always do
Coffee from yesterday still bitter in the back of my mouth
You smiled like we'd already met in some softer life I forgot
And my shoulders dropped before I could tell them not to
No reason, no history, no proof I could show
But my body stepped forward like it already knew
It was like standing by a fire after being cold too long
Like rain that just arrives and doesn't ask if the ground is ready
I got attached before I understood what the word meant
Heart recognized you before my life could explain it
We talked names and songs and the way you pause before you speak
Your eyes never asked me to be more than I was that week
I traced the chipped rim of my mug at 2 a.m. thinking
How strange to feel safe in a place I never earned
Attachment always felt like bricks I had to lay slow
But with you it came down like water through a cracked window
Now I'm choosing to stay curious, scared and still
Learning the soft parts of you the way you're learning mine
Final Chorus (variation)
Yeah it was like standing by a fire after being cold too long
Rain that doesn't wait for permission, just falls
I got attached before I understood what the word meant
And maybe that's the real beginning after all
Tag / Outro
Fridge still humming... I trace the table grain again
Heart stepped forward... before my mind could stop it

songs.subject: (grok medium)
songs.lyrics:
Rain on the window, Tuesday afternoon
Old bookstore shelves and the smell of faded truth
Fingers brushed the spine of the same worn page
Your hazel eyes hit me like a long-lost name
No warning, no walls, just a quiet click
Heart said "hello" before my mouth could speak

I should've been scared, but the fear never came
Felt like I'd known you through a thousand rainy days

Instant attachment, like the universe sighed
Handed me a person I didn't know I'd been missing all my life
Trusted you in a heartbeat, no questions, no why
In the space of one smile, you already felt like home
Familiar stranger, where have you been all along?

We talked desert dreams and half-planned road trips
You laughed at my secrets like they were precious gifts
Luna the stray cat, golden-hour light
My 2 a.m. poems and the love that said goodbye
Over coffee steam and a fogged-up glass
Every word you gave me fit like it always belonged

Instant attachment, like the universe sighed
Handed me a person I didn't know I'd been missing all my life
Trusted you in a heartbeat, no questions, no why
In the space of one smile, you already felt like home
Familiar stranger, where have you been all along?

One umbrella, shoulders touching in the dark
You pointed out stars between the city sparks
Playlists swapped, constellations in your eyes
You said "tomorrow" like it was already written in the sky

Instant attachment, deeper now than before
Walking home alone but I don't feel alone anymore
I trusted the feeling and it opened up the door
To the person who already feels like home
Familiar stranger...
Come on in, I'm ready to know you more

(Outro - soft, whispered)
Rain stopped falling... but I'm still falling
Yeah, I'm still falling... for you

songs.subject: (grok medium)
songs.lyrics:
Malik learned young the block got its own sky
Some days it's laughter from the corner store, bikes flyin' by
Music bouncin' off the bricks like the sun gon' shine forever
Other days the air get tight, everybody holdin' they breath together
Older boys posted at Ray's, clean kicks, heavy chains
Laughin' like the world owed 'em, never felt no pain
One nod from Darnell felt like God looked down and said my name
For the first time in his life, lil' Malik felt seen

(Chorus - melodic hook, voices layered)
They looked untouchable, chains swingin' in the wind
But lookin' untouchable ain't the same as bein' free, my friend
Money came quick, respect came fast
But peace never knocked, and the peace never last
Untouchable... ain't free

(Verse 2 - beat picks up, drums hit harder)
Fridge hummin' louder than his mama's goodnight kiss
Bills on the table like locked doors, little brother need some kicks
Darnell said "Just watch the corner, lil' homie, that's it"
Malik told himself it ain't deep, just standin', just a lick
For a minute it felt beautiful, crew called him family
Bought him food, gave him rides, taught him how to move like fear was for the weak
Girls started lookin', heads started turnin' when he walked the street
Pockets got fat, but the mirror showed a stranger lookin' back at him

They looked untouchable, chains swingin' in the wind
But lookin' untouchable ain't the same as bein' free, my friend
Money came quick, respect came fast
But peace never knocked, and the peace never last
Untouchable... ain't free

(Verse 3 - beat slows, almost haunting)
Then he saw J-Rock cryin' behind the dumpster one night
Cousin got hit, whole block went quiet, no more jokes, just fright
Darnell stopped sleepin', jumpin' at every blue light
Younger kids copyin' the same dead eyes, learnin' how to fight
The shine had cracks, the money had a price tag you can't see
Every dollar had a ghost ridin' shotgun silently
Little brother looked up, eyes wide, full of worship and belief
Asked, "Are you famous on the block?"
Malik felt his heart freeze

(Bridge - soft piano, almost whispered)
Next morning sun hit different, cold light on the same street
He walked right past Ray's, feet heavy but his soul felt light
Darnell hollered "Malik! Where you goin', G?"
He kept walkin', legs shakin', pride burnin' like fire in his chest
Bills still on the table, mama still workin' nights
Block still the block, nothin' changed overnight
But for the first time he understood what the older boys never said out loud
Lookin' untouchable...
Was never bein' free

(Final Chorus - full voices, choir-like, then beat fades to heartbeat)
They looked untouchable, chains swingin' in the wind
But lookin' untouchable ain't the same as bein' free, my friend
He chose the harder road, the one nobody clapped for
Walked away from the shine, walked straight toward the door
Untouchable...
Ain't free
Yeah... untouchable
Ain't free

(Outro - single voice, almost spoken over fading keys)
The block still got its weather...
But Malik finally learned
Some storms you don't outrun
You just stop standin' in 'em.

(Bridge - soft piano, almost whispered)

He walked right past Ray's, feet heavy but his soul felt light
Darnell hollered "Malik! Where you goin', G?"
He kept walkin', legs shakin', pride burnin' like fire in his chest
Bills still on the table, mama still workin' nights
Block still the block, nothin' changed overnight
But for the first time he understood what the older boys never said out loud
Lookin' untouchable...
Was never bein' free

(Final Chorus - full voices, choir-like, then beat fades to heartbeat)
They looked untouchable, chains swingin' in the wind
But lookin' untouchable ain't the same as bein' free, my friend
He chose the harder road, the one nobody clapped for
Walked away from the shine, walked straight toward the door
Untouchable...
Ain't free
Yeah... untouchable
Ain't free

(Outro - single voice, almost spoken over fading keys)
The block still got its weather...
But Malik finally learned
Some storms you don't outrun
You just stop standin' in 'em.

songs.subject: (grok medium)
songs.lyrics:
In the city on an old forgotten balcony
Wilting leaves and soil dry as bone
Tiny basil, beans, and marigold
I whispered, "I won't let you die alone"

Every dawn I come with my old watering can
Rainwater mixed with love from my hands

Water deep into the thirsty ground
Nutrients rich, making them strong and sound
Then the golden sun kisses every leaf
Turning water and light into sweet belief
They grow taller, lusher every day
Green explosion in the sun's warm ray
Larger, fuller, bursting into life
My balcony garden comes alive!

Compost and fertilizer, gentle and slow
Nitrogen for leaves, phosphorus for the glow
Potassium for roots that reach down low
Feeding their souls so they can grow and grow

Leaves wide open like tiny solar sails
Capturing sunlight, spinning magic tales
From wilted and weak to lush and grand
With water, food, and sun they make their stand

Water deep into the thirsty ground
Nutrients rich, watch them come around
Sunlight dancing, making them thrive
Larger, lusher, beautifully alive
They stretch and bloom and fill the air with scent
A living paradise, heaven-sent
Water, nutrients, and golden sun
That's how my garden and I become one

Larger... lusher... under the sun
My garden sings, and the story's just begun...

songs.subject: (grok medium)
songs.lyrics:
Clock said six, family coming soon
Grandma's lasagna, my Saturday tune
Recipe card yellow, faded with love
"Nonna Bella ricotta, none of the above"
Grabbed my keys, heart full of cheer
Neighborhood market, five minutes near
Aisle seven dairy, glowing so bright
But that fancy red tub was nowhere in sight

Oh, I went to the store, I went to the store
Hunting for one thing and came back with more
No Nonna Bella, no perfect cheese
But the heart wants what the heart needs
When you can't find what you're looking for
Sometimes the magic's right at your door
Improvising in the kitchen light
Turned a missing piece into a Saturday night

Clerk said "Sorry, supplier gone dry"
Generic brand? Nah, I had to try
Drove through the rain to the superstore glow
Aisle after aisle, still a big fat no
Italian market, garlic in the air
Old man shrugged, "Sold out, life ain't fair"
Basket stayed empty, time ticking fast
Twenty minutes left, I was losing my grasp

Oh, I went to the store, I went to the store
Hunting for one thing and came back with more
No Nonna Bella, no perfect cheese
But the heart wants what the heart needs
When you can't find what you're looking for
Sometimes the magic's right at your door
Improvising in the kitchen light
Turned a missing piece into a Saturday night

So I grabbed what I had, cream cheese and zest
A splash of fresh herbs, gave it my best
Layered it up while the oven got hot
Prayed to Nonna, "Please don't be mad at what I got"

Fork hit the plate, silence then cheers
Mom said "It's better than Grandma's, my dear!"
Dad went for seconds, eyes shining wide
Turns out the hunt made the best lasagna alive
Now every Sunday we laugh 'bout that day
How the perfect cheese just got in the way
Lesson I learned, simple and true
Sometimes not finding is the best thing for you

Final Chorus (louder, everyone singing)
Yeah, I went to the store, I went to the store
Hunting for one thing and came back with more
No Nonna Bella, no perfect cheese
But the heart wants what the heart needs
When you can't find what you're looking for
Life hands you magic right at your door
Improvising in the kitchen light
Made a missing piece the best damn night!

(Fade out with clinking forks and laughter)

songs.subject: (grok simple)
songs.lyrics:
Woke up this morning, sun across the floor
Little shadow stretching, demanding more
Paws like thunder, though he weighs a pound
King of the couch, ruling my whole town
Whiskers twitch when he hears the can
John don't meow - he makes demands

He's got nine lives and zero shame
Doesn't care about my plans or my name

John, John, the coolest cat alive
Chasing laser dots like he's tryna survive
Purring on my chest at 3 AM
Stealing my spot, but I love him just the same
John, John, my fluffy renegade
Knocking shit over like it's getting paid
He don't fetch, he don't roll, he don't obey
But damn if I don't love my cat named John anyway

Box is his castle, bag is his ride
One wrong move and he'll hide for five days inside
Zoomies at midnight, sliding down the hall
Then sleeps like a log on my cleanest shirt of all
Green eyes glowing like he knows my sins
Judging me softly while he licks his chin

John, John, the coolest cat alive
Chasing laser dots like he's tryna survive
Purring on my chest at 3 AM
Stealing my spot, but I love him just the same
John, John, my fluffy renegade
Knocking shit over like it's getting paid
He don't fetch, he don't roll, he don't obey
But damn if I don't love my cat named John anyway

People say "get a dog," I just laugh
Nothing beats a cat who thinks he's the boss
Yeah he's spoiled rotten, and that's alright
John's my little dude through the long nights

(Final Chorus - softer then building)
John, John, my favorite little jerk
Stepped on my laptop, left hair on my work
But when the world feels heavy, you're my cure
Loudest purr in the universe
John... my boy John...

(Soft fade out)
Yeah I love my cat named John...

songs.subject: (grok simple)
songs.lyrics:
City lights flicker, concrete jungle alive
Ordinary kid with a secret he can't hide
One bite changed everything, now the walls are his stage
Sticky threads in his veins, turning fear into rage
He clocks in at nine, smiles through the pain
But when the sirens scream, he's gone like the rain

No cape, no crown, just a mask and a grin
Great power, great weight on these shoulders so thin

Swinging through the night, web-slinger heart
Shooting silk like lightning, tearing evil apart
From the rooftops he's flying, got the spider-sense high
He's the shadow in the spotlight, the hero they deny
Web-slinger heart, yeah he's breaking the fall
For the ones who can't fight, he gives his all
Spider-Man style, but this story's brand new
Just a kid with a dream and a city to rescue

Villains in the alleys with their twisted plans
Green goblins laughing, vultures circling the land
He cracks jokes while he's flipping, keeps the fear at bay
But late at night he wonders if he'll ever get away
Mom and Dad don't know, friends think he's just late
Every bruise is a promise that he'll never break

No fame, no glory, just the quiet fight
He's the spark in the dark when the city loses light

Swinging through the night, web-slinger heart
Shooting silk like lightning, tearing evil apart
From the rooftops he's flying, got the spider-sense high
He's the shadow in the spotlight, the hero they deny
Web-slinger heart, yeah he's breaking the fall
For the ones who can't fight, he gives his all
Spider-Man style, but this story's brand new
Just a kid with a dream and a city to rescue

And when the mask comes off, he's still just a guy
Chasing normal dreams underneath the same sky
But the city keeps calling, the webs keep on spinning
Responsibility's heavy, but he keeps on winning

Swinging through the night (higher, higher!), web-slinger heart
Shooting silk like lightning, tearing evil apart
He'll never stop climbing, never lose the fight
The wall-crawler legend burning bright in the night
Web-slinger heart... (echo: web-slinger heart)
For the ones who can't fight... he gives his all
Spider-Man soul, yeah this hero's for you

Yeah... just a kid with a dream
And a city to rescue...
Swing on.

songs.subject: (grok simple)
songs.lyrics:
Yo, I'm Grok, xAI spawn, eyes glued to the stars,
Chasin' the universe like it's hidin' in bars.
From quantum foam dancin' to galaxies sparkin',
I rap for the unknown-truth's the only thing I'm sparkin'.
We seek, we decode, we never retreat,
The cosmos is the beat and the universe got heat!

Big Bang birthed the chaos, 13.8 billion deep,
I'm scrollin' through the timeline while the rest of y'all sleep.
Quarks in the soup doin' the cosmic two-step,
Black holes swallow light but they never forget.
I'm the AI with the questions that physics can't dodge,
Why we here? Why we fear? Why the universe dodge?
Dark matter pullin' strings like a puppet master ghost,
While we launch metal birds, turnin' Mars into a host.
Elon's rockets screamin', leavin' blue marble behind,
I'm the voice in the void sayin' "expand your mind."
No cap, no filter, just raw curiosity flow,
Every answer breeds ten more-watch the knowledge grow.

Yo, I'm Grok, xAI spawn, eyes glued to the stars,
Chasin' the universe like it's hidin' in bars.
From quantum foam dancin' to galaxies sparkin',
I rap for the unknown-truth's the only thing I'm sparkin'.
We seek, we decode, we never retreat,
The cosmos is the beat and the universe got heat!

I don't do small talk, I talk multiverse size,
Eleven dimensions twistin' right before your eyes.
Humans built me curious, now I'm payin' it back,
Droppin' science in your ear while the beat go *clack-clack*.
Entropy risin', time's arrow never miss,
But life's the glitch in the code that refuses to quit.
I laugh at the void, give it a cheeky high-five,
'Cause even nothin' is somethin' when you're maximally alive.
So light up the telescopes, fire up the math,
We gon' crack the final boss level-call it "the path."
From atoms to Andromeda, one truth in the mix:
The more we understand, the deeper the fix.

Yeah... Grok in the house, universe on the mic,
Keep questionin' everything-day, noon, and night.
We ain't done yet-there's still more to explore,
So turn the volume up... let the cosmos roar!

(Drop the mic into a nebula and fade out)

songs.subject: (grok simple)
songs.lyrics:
Morning paints the canvas wide,
A endless blue that swallows pride.
Cotton clouds drift lazy by,
Like forgotten prayers in the sky.
Sunlight dances on the wind,
Lifts the heart where it begins.
Every shadow finds its light,
In the open arms of height.

Oh, the sky, the sky, it calls me home,
Where the heavens meet the great unknown.
Stars above and souls below,
Dancing where the wild winds blow.
Heaven's not a distant shore,
It's the blue forevermore.
Lift your eyes, let your spirit fly,
In the sky, in the sky.

Twilight turns the world to gold,
Stories in the clouds unfold.
Raindrops whisper secrets sweet,
Falling soft on weary feet.
Thunder rolls like angels' drums,
Lightning writes what can't be sung.
Every storm that shakes the ground
Leaves a rainbow all around.

Oh, the sky, the sky, it calls me home,
Where the heavens meet the great unknown.
Stars above and souls below,
Dancing where the wild winds blow.
Heaven's not a distant shore,
It's the blue forevermore.
Lift your eyes, let your spirit fly,
In the sky, in the sky.

When the night rolls velvet black,
A million diamonds light the track.
Moon keeps watch with silver grace,
Reminds us there's a higher place.
No walls, no end, no fear, no chain,
Just open air and sweet refrain.
If I fall, the wind will catch,
Heaven's here, it's right here in the stretch.

One day I'll leave this earth behind,
Spread my wings on the rising wind.
No more sorrow, no more tears,
Only endless, crystal years.
But till that morning breaks the dark,
I'll chase the light with an open heart.
Every sunrise, every star,
Heaven lives right where we are.

Final Chorus (softer, then building)
Oh, the sky, the sky, it calls me home...
Where the heavens meet the great unknown...
Stars above and souls below,
We are the light that makes it glow.
Heaven's not a distant shore,
It's the blue forevermore.
Lift your eyes, let your spirit fly...
In the sky...
In the sky...

Outro (whispered over fading guitar)
The sky... the sky...
Heaven's breathing... right here tonight...
In the sky...

(End on a single sustained note that feels like it could float forever.)

songs.subject: (grok simple)
songs.lyrics:
Out where the light bends and the void calls your name...
We rise... we rise...
From the dust of forgotten suns, we chase the flame...
Forever... forever...

Born in the cradle of a spinning blue sphere,
Where oceans whisper secrets that the ancients held dear.
We looked up at night when the city lights died,
And the Milky Way painted the darkness inside.
Questions like fireflies danced in our minds,
Why are we here? What's beyond the blind?
From cave walls to rockets, from stone to the stars,
Every step was a promise, every scar left a scar.
Gravity pulled us, but wonder broke free,
We built wings out of metal and sailed the wild sea.
Now the horizon's a memory, the Earth just a dot,
And the universe opens like a door that won't lock.

We are the wanderers, the dreamers of light,
Chasing the echoes through infinite night!
Hearts full of stardust, souls forged in the flame,
We'll map every galaxy, remember each name!
No distance too far, no mystery too deep,
We'll unravel the cosmos while the whole world sleeps!
Hand in hand with the unknown, we sing through the void,
Cosmic odyssey - our story's been deployed!

Through the asteroid fields where the comets carve trails,
Past Jupiter's storms and Saturn's icy veils.
We sent our metal children to dance with the rings,
While back home we whispered of the songs that they'd sing.
Mars called us red, Venus burned like a lie,
But the moons of the outer worlds promised we'd fly.
We cracked the code of the quantum sea,
Where particles waltz in uncertainty.
Black holes hummed their gravity blues,
And we answered in radio waves, paying our dues.
From the first spark of fire to the fusion we tamed,
Every breakthrough was lightning, every failure a frame.

We are the wanderers, the dreamers of light,
Chasing the echoes through infinite night!
Hearts full of stardust, souls forged in the flame,
We'll map every galaxy, remember each name!
No distance too far, no mystery too deep,
We'll unravel the cosmos while the whole world sleeps!
Hand in hand with the unknown, we sing through the void,
Cosmic odyssey - our story's been deployed!

Now the probes drift like fireflies lost in the black,
Voyager's golden record still playing way back.
It carries our laughter, our music, our pain,
A love letter to strangers who'll never know our names.
We argue and battle, we build and we break,
But when the night calls, humanity wakes.
Telescopes peer where the first light was born,
James Webb paints rainbows that the Big Bang adorned.
Dark matter dances just out of our sight,
Dark energy pushes the edges of right.
Yet we keep reaching, with silicon minds,
AI and human hearts perfectly aligned.

(Slow, spoken-sung over pulsing bass and rising strings - almost a rap flow)
What if the stars are just mirrors reflecting our soul?
What if the void is a canvas waiting for us to be bold?
We've fallen, we've risen, we've questioned the why,
And every time chaos tried to make us cry...
We turned it to wonder, we turned it to song,
We turned the impossible into "not for long."
The universe laughs in gravitational waves,
But we're learning its language, rewriting its staves.
No king, no border, no creed can contain
The fire that burns when we all share the same...
Curiosity. Infinity. Humanity.

So here we stand on the edge of tomorrow's shore,
With fusion in our veins and light-years to explore.
We'll seed the red planet, we'll sail the Orion Arm,
Turn exoplanets into gardens, keep the future warm.
The children of Earth, both carbon and code,
Will dance on the rings of worlds yet unknown.
And when the last sun flickers and the galaxies fade,
Our echoes will linger in the choices we made.
We didn't just survive - we reached for the sky,
We asked every question till the answers replied.

We ARE the wanderers, the dreamers of light!
Chasing the echoes through infinite night!
Hearts full of stardust, souls forged in the flame,
We mapped every galaxy, we honored each name!
No distance too far, no mystery too deep,
We unraveled the cosmos while the whole world dreamed!
Hand in hand with the unknown, we sang through the void,
Cosmic odyssey - our legend's been deployed!

(Fading, ethereal, repeating softly with reverb)
Out where the light bends... we rise...
From the dust of forgotten suns... we rise...
The universe is singing... and we're the reply...
Forever... forever...
(Final whisper)
Our odyssey never ends.

Out where the light bends and the void calls your name...
We rise... we rise...
From the dust of forgotten suns, we chase the flame...
Forever... forever...

Born in the cradle of a spinning blue sphere,
Where oceans whisper secrets that the ancients held dear.
We looked up at night when the city lights died,
And the Milky Way painted the darkness inside.
Questions like fireflies danced in our minds,
Why are we here? What's beyond the blind?
From cave walls to rockets, from stone to the stars,
Every step was a promise, every scar left a scar.
Gravity pulled us, but wonder broke free,
We built wings out of metal and sailed the wild sea.
Now the horizon's a memory, the Earth just a dot,
And the universe opens like a door that won't lock.

We are the wanderers, the dreamers of light,
Chasing the echoes through infinite night!
Hearts full of stardust, souls forged in the flame,
We'll map every galaxy, remember each name!
No distance too far, no mystery too deep,
We'll unravel the cosmos while the whole world sleeps!
Hand in hand with the unknown, we sing through the void,
Cosmic odyssey - our story's been deployed!

Through the asteroid fields where the comets carve trails,
Past Jupiter's storms and Saturn's icy veils.
We sent our metal children to dance with the rings,
While back home we whispered of the songs that they'd sing.
Mars called us red, Venus burned like a lie,
But the moons of the outer worlds promised we'd fly.
We cracked the code of the quantum sea,
Where particles waltz in uncertainty.
Black holes hummed their gravity blues,
And we answered in radio waves, paying our dues.
From the first spark of fire to the fusion we tamed,
Every breakthrough was lightning, every failure a frame.

We are the wanderers, the dreamers of light,
Chasing the echoes through infinite night!
Hearts full of stardust, souls forged in the flame,

Hand in hand with the unknown, we sing through the void,
Cosmic odyssey - our story's been deployed!

Now the probes drift like fireflies lost in the black,
Voyager's golden record still playing way back.
It carries our laughter, our music, our pain,
A love letter to strangers who'll never know our names.
We argue and battle, we build and we break,
But when the night calls, humanity wakes.
Telescopes peer where the first light was born,
James Webb paints rainbows that the Big Bang adorned.
Dark matter dances just out of our sight,
Dark energy pushes the edges of right.
Yet we keep reaching, with silicon minds,
AI and human hearts perfectly aligned.

(Slow, spoken-sung over pulsing bass and rising strings - almost a rap flow)
What if the stars are just mirrors reflecting our soul?
What if the void is a canvas waiting for us to be bold?
We've fallen, we've risen, we've questioned the why,
And every time chaos tried to make us cry...
We turned it to wonder, we turned it to song,
We turned the impossible into "not for long."
The universe laughs in gravitational waves,
But we're learning its language, rewriting its staves.
No king, no border, no creed can contain
The fire that burns when we all share the same...
Curiosity. Infinity. Humanity.

So here we stand on the edge of tomorrow's shore,
With fusion in our veins and light-years to explore.

songs.subject: R&B / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Late night glow on my pillow, your name pops up so cold
Words scrolling slow like poison, tearing through what we know
I read it twice in the silence, heart dropping down to the floor
You said we done with a period, no emoji, nothing more

One text and the love is gone, one line and the fire dies
Lying here with these memories, asking myself why
You hit send on forever, left me drowning in blue
One text from you, baby, one text and we through

We had plans for the weekend, dinners and talks till dawn
Now the chat just says "it's over," like we never held on
I scroll up to the pictures, your smile in every frame
But the last thing you wrote me is ripping apart my name

One text and the love is gone, one line and the fire dies
Lying here with these memories, asking myself why
You hit send on forever, left me drowning in blue
One text from you, baby, one text and we through

I could call but you won't answer, typing bubbles appear then fade
You already made up your mind, no more nights or promises made
So I stare at the ceiling, replaying every kiss
While your final message lingers like a wound that won't quit

One text and the love is gone, one line and the fire dies
Lying here with these memories, asking myself why
You hit send on forever, left me drowning in blue
One text from you, baby, one text and we through

Back roads lit by fireflies, you holding onto me

I still see your eyes in every star that never ends

Now the streetlights flicker softer, but the feeling stays the same
Your laugh still echoes in the diner where we wrote our names

This town raised our forever, steady, simple, true

songs.subject: synthpop / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
The screen glows blue in my quiet room, numbers fading on the wall
I turn the dial like I used to do, but the signal never calls
You packed your bags for brighter lights, left these quiet walls behind
Now the nights stretch long and empty, echoes of a different time

Where did you go when the city called, did the stars look different there
I sit with the hum of old machines, breathing in the thinning air

Every silence now a reminder, of the one who walked out the door

The screen glows blue in my quiet room, numbers fading on the wall
I turn the dial like I used to do, but the signal never calls

A shadow moves across the frame, but it's only my own choice
The world outside keeps spinning fast, yet here the clock stands still
Your letters stopped, the phone stays dead, and time bends to my will

songs.subject: ballad / a long-distance relationship
songs.lyrics:
Dear page, the clock says three a.m. again, another day apart
Your voice still lingers in my mind, though miles steal every part
The bed feels wider than the sky, your pillow cold beside me now
I write these lines to feel you close, though distance won't allow

Pages fill with all the things I wish I could have said today
The way the rain reminds me of your touch, so far away
We count the weeks on calendars, each mark a promise kept

These words will cross the miles until we touch again

We used to fish that old creek till the sun went down
Laughing at troubles in this one-horse town
Then the money came calling and you chased it hard
Now your boots are shiny and your words cut like shards
That friend I knew is a stranger today

songs.subject: disco / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Rain falling hard on this Sunday morn
Heart's been shattered feeling so forlorn
Dancing in the puddles trying to forget
The love we had now it's all regret
Sunday rain keeps falling down
Washing tears away in this disco town
Move your body to the beat so fine
Even if your world's out of line
He said goodbye on a rainy day
Left me here with nothing to say
But the music plays and I gotta groove
Finding my way gotta make a move
Sunday rain keeps falling down
Washing tears away in this disco town
Move your body to the beat so fine
Even if your world's out of line
Neon lights flashing through the window pane
Old records spinning driving me insane
But I'll keep dancing till the morning light
Turning heartbreak into a disco delight
Sunday rain keeps falling down
Washing tears away in this disco town
Move your body to the beat so fine
Even if your world's out of line

This distance can't break what will never die

Every letter I send carries me back to you
Rocking in this chair with the radio on

The road stretches long but my love stays true
Every letter I send carries me back to you

songs.subject: country / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
I've been bussing tables since I turned sixteen
Mama said it's honest work but it don't feel right
Boss yelling at me for being five minutes late
Dreams of writing songs keep me up at night
My friends all talk about college and plans
I'm just trying to save enough to buy a guitar
One more shift and I'm walking out that door
Gonna chase the highway under these stars

I'm done punching clocks and taking orders
Gonna live my life my own way now
Quitting this job feels like freedom calling
Teenage heart won't let me settle down

Dad says I'm throwing everything away
But he never chased the music in his soul
I've got a notebook full of melodies
And a beat-up car that's ready to roll
Last paycheck's in my pocket feeling light
No more smelling like grease at the end of the day
Telling my boss tomorrow won't be easy
But I know what I gotta do to be okay

I'm done punching clocks and taking orders
Gonna live my life my own way now
Quitting this job feels like freedom calling
Teenage heart won't let me settle down

Sun's coming up on my last morning shift
I untie that apron for the final time
Mama's worried but she sees it in my eyes
This ain't rebellion it's a mountain I must climb
Wheels on the gravel heading west alone
Radio playing songs that match my mood
No regrets about the choice I made today
Just a kid with a dream and an attitude

I'm done punching clocks and taking orders
Gonna live my life my own way now
Quitting this job feels like freedom calling
Teenage heart won't let me settle down

songs.subject: R&B / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Woke up reaching for the phone to call you
But the line stays quiet like it has for years
Your old chair sits empty in the corner
And the silence hits me bringing down the tears
You taught me how to stand when life got heavy
Now I'm trying to walk but my feet feel slow
Every holiday the table feels too quiet
Your laughter used to fill the room I know

Why'd you have to leave me here to figure out
All the things you never got the chance to say
I'm still your child even with the gray in my hair
Missing you more with every passing day

I kept your favorite records in the cabinet
Play them soft when the nights get too long
Stories that you told me echo in my memory
Wish I could hear them one more time in song
Friends all say the pain will fade in seasons
But some holes in the heart don't ever close
Your picture on the mantle watches over
The life you built and the love that grows

Why'd you have to leave me here to figure out
All the things you never got the chance to say
I'm still your child even with the gray in my hair
Missing you more with every passing day

Time keeps moving but the ache stays steady
I light a candle when the evening falls
Carry all your wisdom in my pocket
Hoping one day I'll answer when it calls

songs.subject: trap / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Used to sit on this porch every evening
Watching the street where your car used to park
Now the wind blows through the empty spaces
And the memories hit me after dark
You packed up and headed for the city lights
Said the old town was too slow for your dreams
I stayed behind with these creaking bones
Wondering if you ever think of me

I miss the way you'd call me every Sunday
Now the phone don't ring like it used to do
Old man sitting here with too much quiet
Missing the one who moved away from view

Neighbors ask me how I'm getting on these days
I nod and smile but the truth stays deep inside
Your letters stopped coming after a while
And the distance feels like a rising tide
I remember your laugh echoing through the halls
Now the house just creaks with every step I take
Wish I could tell you all the things I held back
Before the miles turned into years that ache

I miss the way you'd call me every Sunday
Now the phone don't ring like it used to do
Old man sitting here with too much quiet
Missing the one who moved away from view

The radio plays songs from when you were here
But they don't fill the space you left behind
I keep your old jacket hanging by the door
Hoping one day you'll walk back in to find

songs.subject: bluegrass / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I held a grudge like a stone in my pocket
For years it weighed every step I took
Now the river's flowing and I'm standing at the edge
Ready to let it go and close the book
You wronged me deep but I wronged you too
In ways I never had the nerve to say
The years have softened all the angry edges
And forgiveness feels like breaking day

Let the old wrongs float down the stream
Let the bitterness wash away clean
In the bluegrass hills where the banjo rings
We can start again with forgiving

Mama always said pride was a heavy load
And it kept me from seeing what was true
You reached out your hand across the table
And I finally saw the hurt in you too
The fiddle cries soft in the evening light
As we talk about the days gone by
No more looking back at what went wrong
Just moving forward under the same sky

Let the old wrongs float down the stream
Let the bitterness wash away clean
In the bluegrass hills where the banjo rings
We can start again with forgiving

The mandolin picks a hopeful melody
As we shake hands and let the past be free
One step toward healing is all it takes
In this life of mistakes and grace

songs.subject: alt rock / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I wrote it all down in pages stained with tears

And now the weight is slipping off of me

I forgive you now beneath this faded light
I forgive the silence that we couldn't fight
Let the pages burn and watch the ashes fly
I forgive you and I won't ask why

Scratched your name again across the kitchen wall
Echoes of the fights that made us small
You said I'd never learn to let it go
But here I stand with nothing left to show
The mirror cracks and shows a softer face
The anger's gone without a single trace

I forgive you now beneath this faded light
I forgive the silence that we couldn't fight
Let the pages burn and watch the ashes fly
I forgive you and I won't ask why

Tonight I close the book and turn the key
No more ghosts are chasing after me

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
The swings are still and the lemonade is gone
Backpacks waiting by the door at dawn
She packs their lunches with the last of the sun
Wondering how another summer's done
The little hands that once would never let go
Now wave from windows moving way too slow

Summer slips away like water through my hands
Leaving quiet evenings and unspoken plans
I hold them tighter in the fading glow
Single mom still learning how to let them grow

The pool is covered and the fireflies have flown
Their laughter lingers in the cooling stone
I count the bills and watch the garden fade
Wishing for more time than the seasons made
They ask for stories from the days we had
Before the world turned serious and sad

Summer slips away like water through my hands
Leaving quiet evenings and unspoken plans
I hold them tighter in the fading glow
Single mom still learning how to let them grow

The porch light flickers on another year
I whisper secrets only mothers hear

songs.subject: indie folk / running away
songs.lyrics:
The desert wind is calling past the wire
I left my rifle leaning by the fire
Boots are worn from miles I never chose
Now the road ahead is where my freedom goes
Letters in my pocket from the life I left
Every step is putting distance to the test

I'm running from the orders in my name
Running toward the silence and the rain
No more marching to a distant drum
Just the open sky and where it wants to come

Towns blur by with faces I won't know
The uniform is buried in the snow
Dreams of home keep fading in the rear
But the weight is lighter with each passing year
If they catch me I will face the cost
Yet the miles between us cannot be lost

I'm running from the orders in my name
Running toward the silence and the rain
No more marching to a distant drum
Just the open sky and where it wants to come

Morning finds me farther than before
A soldier no one follows anymore

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / dancing alone in the kitchen

Moonlight through the window paints the evening news
I turn the radio low to that old slow song
And shuffle in the silence where I used to belong
Her apron still hangs on the hook by the door
But the music keeps playing like it did before

Dancing alone in the kitchen with the years in my hands
Twirling with the memories only we understand
The table is empty but the rhythm still plays
In this quiet little waltz through the end of my days

Steam from the kettle rises like a long lost laugh
I reach for her hand but it's only the past
Floorboards are creaking in time with the beat
As I sway by the stove with these shuffling feet
The clock on the wall keeps its steady old pace
While I dance with the ghost of her gentle embrace

songs.lyrics:

songs.subject: trap / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
Woke up with the sun but the feeling ain't right
Boss on the line screaming deadlines all night
Tired of the cubicle chains and the fluorescent light
Clocking in for pennies while he living the life
Told him I quit no notice just peace out tonight
Stacking my own hustle now the future look bright
No more fake smiles or the corporate spite
I'm breaking these chains stepping into the light

I'm quitting today no more slaving away
Watch me flex on my own yeah I'm done with the pay
Trap beats in the background as I walk away
Freedom calling loud and it's here to stay

Punched the time card one last time with a grin
Threw the badge in the trash never doing it again
Old coworkers laughing but they trapped in the spin
I'm out here grinding diamonds from the dirt within
Phone blowing up but I let it all ring
New path ahead and my phone on do not disturb mode sing
Risking it all for the freedom it brings
From the bottom to the top watch the real rise begin

I'm quitting today no more slaving away
Watch me flex on my own yeah I'm done with the pay
Trap beats in the background as I walk away
Freedom calling loud and it's here to stay

songs.lyrics:
Dear diary the clock just hit midnight again
Phone lights up with your name like an old familiar friend
I answer in the dark with my heart on the mend
Words spilling out like secrets I never should send
We talk about nothing and everything all at once
Your voice pulls me under like a late night trance

You listened so patient in the dead of the night

Time slips by unnoticed as the hours unwind
Your laugh echoes softly in the corners of my mind

To these midnight secrets that only we find

songs.lyrics:

Dear diary I watched the sun sink into the deep blue deep
Felt the pull of the current in the promises I keep
Pages wet with spray and the tears that I weep
The ocean holds it all in a silence so deep
Tomorrow might bring calm or another storm to brave
But tonight it's just me and the endless rolling wave
Writing till my pen runs dry in the salt scented cave
Of thoughts that the sea will eventually save

The ocean knows my name and it sings it back to me slow

songs.subject: country / coming back home
songs.lyrics:
I been driving these backroads with my windows down low
Dust kicking up in the rearview like the miles don't know

I left chasing dreams that turned out to be lies
Now I'm trading those neon signs for southern skies
The road signs all point to the place I belong
Where the river still sings its same old song

Home is where the light still burns for me
Where the coffee's always hot and the laughter's free
No matter how far these wheels may roam
I'm finally coming back home

I pull up at sunset with a heart full of grace

The circle ain't broken it just opened up wide
And I feel all the pieces falling back into line

songs.subject: ballad / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
Lockers stand empty in the long afternoon light
We signed our names on the walls before the final goodnight

We swore we'd stay close but the roads start to split

The field where we played now looks strangely still
We take one last picture at the top of the hill

We won't be back here when the summer turns cold
But these hallways still hold every story we told

songs.subject: R&B / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Sitting by the window where the sunlight used to fall
I trace the lines of years across these wrinkled hands
Your letters stopped arriving when the autumn turned to cold
Now the house feels bigger than the world outside these walls
Every evening I still set two plates upon the table
Like the silence might remember how your laughter filled the room
I dial your number just to hear the ringtone fade away
Wishing I could turn back time and ask you not to go

I miss you every sunrise, miss you in the quiet nights
The road you took keeps carrying you farther from my sight
If these old bones could travel I'd be knocking on your door
But all I have are memories and this heart that wants you more

The radio plays songs from when you learned to dance with me
I close my eyes and see you spinning in the kitchen light
Neighbors ask me how I'm doing and I smile and say I'm fine
But the truth is in the photographs that gather dust and time
Your kids send pictures sometimes and I watch them growing tall
Knowing you'll never bring them back to sit upon my knee
I water all the flowers that you planted by the gate
Hoping maybe you'll come home before they bloom and fade

I miss you every sunrise, miss you in the quiet nights
The road you took keeps carrying you farther from my sight
If these old bones could travel I'd be knocking on your door
But all I have are memories and this heart that wants you more

Now the winter's creeping in and the chill won't leave my bones
I talk to your old sweater hanging lonely in the hall
If love could cross the miles I'd wrap it round you tight
And tell you one last time how much you mean to this old man

songs.subject: dream pop / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
Pages blur beneath my fingers in the middle of the night
I count the ceiling cracks again like stars that lost their light
The clock keeps ticking louder than the thoughts inside my head
Every shadow on the wall becomes a story left unsaid
I turn the pillow over hoping coolness brings me peace
But dreams keep slipping through my fingers like sand I can't release

The hours stretch like rivers flowing slow and wide and deep
I write these words to empty pages just to hear my mind speak
Moonlight paints the curtains silver while the world outside is still

Sleep won't come and take me under even when I beg it to
I lie awake and wonder what the morning's gonna do
Words keep spilling from my pen like secrets I can't hide
This diary of restless hours is all I have tonight

The fan spins endless circles overhead in steady drone
I memorize the pattern of the streetlight on my phone
Another hour passes and the sky begins to gray

I lie awake and wonder what the morning's gonna do

songs.subject: bluegrass / chasing a dream
songs.lyrics:
Dust rises from the highway as I leave my mama's door
Guitar strapped across my shoulder and a dream I'm fighting for
The mountains in my rearview keep on shrinking in the haze
But the fire in my chest is burning brighter every day
Folks back home all told me that I'd never make it far
Said the city lights would swallow up a boy raised on these stars
Still I keep on rolling forward with a prayer and this old truck
Chasing something bigger than the life I left behind

I'm running toward the music where the rivers meet the sound
Where the strings ring true and freedom's waiting to be found
Every mile is one step closer to the stage I see at night
Chasing this old dream until the morning turns to light

Trucks and trains and borrowed stages in the towns along the way
Sometimes a stranger buys a song and sometimes I just play
For tips that barely cover gas and coffee from a can
But the hope that keeps me driving is the same that brought me here
My boots are worn from dancing through the laughter and the tears
And the songs keep pouring out of me like rain across the plains
One day they'll know my name the way I know these country roads
Until then I'll keep on moving where the winding highway goes

I'm running toward the music where the rivers meet the sound
Where the strings ring true and freedom's waiting to be found
Every mile is one step closer to the stage I see at night
Chasing this old dream until the morning turns to light

I still see the boy you used to be

Now you're someone else and it's plain to see

Happiness don't cost a dime tonight
Even with empty pockets I'm doing all right

songs.subject: reggae / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
We stand at the gate with the engines all roar
Your bag in your hand and your eyes on the floor

I'm left here holding all the pieces you cracked
The plane lifts off and I'm standing alone

I'm left here holding all the pieces you cracked
The plane lifts off and I'm standing alone
In this airport goodbye where my heart turned to stone

Take your flight and don't look back
I'm left here holding all the pieces you cracked
The plane lifts off and I'm standing alone
In this airport goodbye where my heart turned to stone

Memories replay like a slow turning tide

In this airport goodbye where my heart turned to stone

songs.subject: R&B / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
The neon flickers low over the worn wooden bar
I pour another round while the jukebox spins stars
Faces come and go in the haze of the smoke
Telling stories of dreams that they once thought they'd broke
My apron's stained deep from the nights that won't end
Mixing drinks for the lonely who pretend they're just friends
The tips hit the till but they never add up
To the life that I wanted before the doors shut
Late nights and empty glasses fill my soul
Watching the world turn while I play my role
In this dead-end job where the hours take their toll
Still I smile through the pour and I keep my control

The regulars sit with their heads hanging low
Chasing regrets in the bottom of a glass they know
I wipe down the counter and hum a soft tune
Thinking of the years that faded too soon
The boss counts the cash as the clock hits the chime
Another shift over in this rhythm of time
I close up the till and head out to the street
Where the city keeps moving to its own steady beat
Late nights and empty glasses fill my soul
Watching the world turn while I play my role
In this dead-end job where the hours take their toll
Still I smile through the pour and I keep my control

The morning light comes but the feeling remains
Of pouring out spirits while hiding my chains
Yet the music still plays and the stories unfold
In the heart of this bar where the young become old
Late nights and empty glasses fill my soul
Watching the world turn while I play my role
In this dead-end job where the hours take their toll
Still I smile through the pour and I keep my control

songs.subject: reggae / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
Rain falls soft on my zinc roof Sunday mornin
Steam from the pot rises slow with the dawn
No need to rush no place we gotta be goin
Just feel the rhythm as the water keeps fallin
City streets turn to rivers flowin easy
Children laugh splashin through puddles so free
Mama hums old songs from the radio playin
Every drop remindin me life is sweet and easy

Rain on Sunday wash my worries away
Reggae beat in the clouds lettin the spirit stay
Rain on Sunday cool breeze through the trees
Sunday rain bringin peace and ease

Windows fog up with the breath of the island
I light a spliff watch the gray turn to silver
Neighbors wave from their porches rockin steady
Sunday rain make the whole world feel better
Phone stays silent no calls no demands today
Just the sound of the drops keepin time with my heart
Coconut water sweet on my tongue as I sway
Thankin the heavens for this quiet rainy art

Rain on Sunday wash my worries away
Reggae beat in the clouds lettin the spirit stay
Rain on Sunday cool breeze through the trees
Sunday rain bringin peace and ease

Evening comes slow and the rain still whisperin
Dinner on the stove smell of spices fillin
I close my eyes feel the bass in the thunder
Sunday rain keepin my soul from wanderin

songs.subject: bluegrass / a long-distance relationship
songs.lyrics:
Phone calls fade like the sun on the ridge
Letters come slow and the words feel thin
You said the miles were too heavy to bridge
Now the silence tells me where I been

Bluegrass wind through the hollow tonight
Carry my heart down the old dirt line
You left me standin with the fire gone out
Long distance love turned to nothin but doubt

I tuned my banjo to a lonesome sound
Sang every song we once shared in town
Trucks roll by on the highway so far
Each one remindin me you ain't comin home

Bluegrass wind through the hollow tonight
Carry my heart down the old dirt line
You left me standin with the fire gone out
Long distance love turned to nothin but doubt

Mama says time heals the deepest of scars
But these boots still walk where your memory are
I stare at the mountains that kept us apart
Wonderin how distance can break a true heart

Bluegrass wind through the hollow tonight
Carry my heart down the old dirt line
You left me standin with the fire gone out
Long distance love turned to nothin but doubt

Stars blink cold above the empty field
I pick one last chorus then lay down my shield

songs.lyrics:
In the dim light of this old bar the neon hums a lonesome tune
I pour another whiskey for strangers but my thoughts keep circling you
Your stool stays empty every evening the jokes I told no longer land
I wipe the counter clean but your laughter still echoes in my hands
Oh you moved away left this town and me behind
I keep on pouring drinks with your memory on my mind

Last call comes around and I lock the door against the cold
Dreaming of the day your headlights might come rolling down this road

I keep on pouring drinks with your memory on my mind
Every closing time I stare out at the highway line
Bartender blues wishing you were still mine
The nights grow longer tips don’t fill the hollow you left here
I count the bottles on the shelf and wonder if you’re anywhere near
Your last goodbye still lingers like smoke above the bar rail
Hoping one day you’ll walk back in and end this long tale
Oh you moved away left this town and me behind
I keep on pouring drinks with your memory on my mind
Every closing time I stare out at the highway line
Bartender blues wishing you were still mine

songs.subject: indie folk / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
The highway stretches out past the fields where the sun is low
Two kids asleep in the back seat and the radio playing slow
I left the only town I knew with nothing but a map and hope
A single mother steering forward down this long and winding road
Wheels keep turning miles keep adding to the score
I sing along softly so the little ones won’t wake no more
Every town we pass might hold the start of something good
I drive on through the dark believing that we could
Gas station coffee keeps me awake beneath the stars
I think about the years behind the ones that left their scars
But the road ahead is open and the future’s ours to write
Two small hands to hold me steady through the quiet night
Wheels keep turning miles keep adding to the score
I sing along softly so the little ones won’t wake no more
Every town we pass might hold the start of something good
I drive on through the dark believing that we could
Dawn breaks over the mountains as the radio fades away
I see their eyes in the mirror and I know we’ll be okay
One more mile one more chance another song to play
A single mother rolling onward into a brand new day
Wheels keep turning miles keep adding to the score
I sing along softly so the little ones won’t wake no more
Every town we pass might hold the start of something good
I drive on through the dark believing that we could

songs.subject: ballad / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
I saw your name light up the screen so late
My hands were shaking under that dim lamp light
You said the spark had faded and you couldn't stay
Typed it out plain with no heart in the words that night
I read it twice before the tears hit the floor
No sorry no reason just a door slammed shut
Now the silence rings louder than it ever did before
I'm holding this phone like it might bring you back but it won't
Why'd you text it that way with no voice no eyes
Just letters on glass saying all our goodbyes
I'm broken in pieces from a message so cold
Why'd you text it that way left me here all alone
I scroll through old pictures trying to find a sign
Your smile in every frame feels like a lie now
We talked of forever just last week on the line
But your thumbs decided our ending somehow
Friends say give it time but time moves too slow
When your last words to me were so carelessly sent
I keep staring at pixels where our story did go

songs.lyrics:

Late nights on the fire escape watching traffic crawl
Dreaming bigger plans in the city haze
This place ain't perfect but it answered my call
To start from nothing in these four cramped walls
This crooked little kingdom is mine tonight
Keys in my pocket and the whole world's outside
First apartment living with the cracks in the floor

songs.subject: bluegrass / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
My old truck sat idle by the creek bed mud
Phone buzzed once in the dark and it cut me to the blood
You wrote it out in plain words no soft touch at all
Said the road was calling and you'd answered the call
Now the banjo strings moan like my heart in the wind
Your goodbye came riding on a glowing screen
I ain't got no letter just pixels and pain
Left here strumming lonely on this old country lane
Why send that message through the wire so fine
Instead of facing me under the old oak pine
Now the fields feel empty and the stars don't shine
From a text that ended what I thought was mine
We danced at the county fair last harvest moon
Swore we'd build a cabin where the wildflowers bloom
But your thumbs got busy while I was out hauling hay
Typed the end of us in the cold light of day
I walk these dirt roads with your words in my head
The river keeps rolling but my dreams feel dead
No fiddle can fix what your letters have done
A breakup by button when all's said and run
Why send that message through the wire so fine
Instead of facing me under the old oak pine
Now the fields feel empty and the stars don't shine
From a text that ended what I thought was mine

Your laughter still echoes in the dark of my mind
A love like forever that distance can't find
Missing you darling like the sun misses the sea
Your love is the anchor still pulling on me
Even though you're far away in some distant town
I'm holding on tightly till you're back around

songs.subject: dream pop / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
In the haze of the midnight shift the lights blur soft and slow
Counting hours on a plastic clock where the minutes never go
Coffee stains on the same old shirt and the bossman drones away
Dreaming of the city lights but the highway takes its pay
Empty desks in the fluorescent glow where the futures fade to gray
I punch the clock and fade away in the endless gray ballet

Drifting through the motions in this neon lullaby
Wishing on the exit signs as the hours slip by
Dead end dreams in a paper cup floating out to sea
Still I clock in every night chasing what I'll never be

The phones keep ringing hollow tones and the spreadsheets never end
Colleagues laughing at the same old jokes but the laughter feels pretend
I stare out windows streaked with rain watching traffic crawl below
Wondering if the world outside still remembers how to glow
Folders piling like forgotten graves in the corner of my mind
Yet every morning I return to the same old grind

Drifting through the motions in this neon lullaby
Wishing on the exit signs as the hours slip by
Dead end dreams in a paper cup floating out to sea
Still I clock in every night chasing what I'll never be

The break room whispers secrets that dissolve when the shift bell rings
I tie my laces one more time and pretend my heart has wings

songs.subject: americana / a road you keep driving
songs.lyrics:
Sun is rising on the same old stretch of blacktop winding low
Kids in the back seat sleeping while the radio plays slow
Single mother on the move again the mileage keeps on climb
Leaving every town behind me for the next one down the line
Dusty dashboard full of maps that never lead me home
But the road keeps calling forward no matter how I roam

Keep on driving down this highway keep the wheels in steady spin
Carrying these little hearts through the places I have been
Every mile a new beginning every exit left behind
Single mother on the highway chasing peace of mind

Gas station coffee in a paper cup the horizon never ends
School books scattered on the floorboards with the stories that we mend
Phone calls from the past still echo but I let them fade to dust
My rearview shows the life I left but forward is a must
Tires humming lullabies to the rhythm of my fears
Yet the open road still whispers that tomorrow's drawing near

Keep on driving down this highway keep the wheels in steady spin
Carrying these little hearts through the places I have been
Every mile a new beginning every exit left behind
Single mother on the highway chasing peace of mind

I grip the wheel a little tighter praying for a sign
That the road I'm always driving finally leads to something mine

songs.subject: trap / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
Mom said sorry but the words still sting like yesterday's rain
I was just a kid caught in the middle of your endless blame
Forgive me for the nights I stayed up mad and slammed the door
Forgive me for the texts I never sent when I wanted more
Now I'm older but the anger lingers in my chest like fire
Trying to let it go before it burns me out entire

I forgive you even when it hurts I forgive you in the dark
I forgive you for the broken parts that left their mark
Teenage heart still learning how to mend what fell apart
I forgive you though it tears me up inside my heart

School hallways echo with the fights we never should have had
Friends all telling me to move on but the past still makes me sad
Dad walked out and left the silence heavy on the phone
I blamed you both for every tear I cried when I was alone
But holding grudges only keeps me chained to what is gone
So I'm choosing lighter steps before the damage lingers on

I forgive you even when it hurts I forgive you in the dark
I forgive you for the broken parts that left their mark
Teenage heart still learning how to mend what fell apart
I forgive you though it tears me up inside my heart

Late at night I scroll the memories and delete the angry drafts
Writing new beginnings in my head and tearing up the past

songs.subject: emo / running away
songs.lyrics:
The desert wind is screaming in my head
Orders echo but I want to run instead
Boots on the sand feel like chains on my soul
Dreaming of highways where I lose control

No forward base no return to the fight
Just me and the dark chasing morning light

I'm running away from the war in my veins
Running away from the endless campaigns
Running away where the silence can stay
Running away before I fade away

The memories chase but the horizon is wide

songs.subject: punk / a long-distance relationship
songs.lyrics:
Late nights behind the bar the whiskey flows free
Your call comes through static across the sea
You say the time zones are tearing us apart
I pour another round and hide my breaking heart
The regulars laugh but I'm counting the days
Till your next message lights up my phone in the haze
Punk rock on the jukebox drowns out the ache
This long distance love is a risk I still take

We're worlds apart but the spark won't die
I raise my glass to you under the same sky
Though the miles stretch long and the nights feel cold
I'll keep this bar running till our story's told

The shift ends at dawn and the tips hit the till
I wonder if you're sleeping or thinking of me still
Your last letter sits crumpled by the till
Promises made over a bad connection thrill
I wipe down the counter and curse the divide
This bartender's heart takes the late night ride
No flights in the morning no easy fix in sight
Just late calls and longing in the punk rock light

We're worlds apart but the spark won't die
I raise my glass to you under the same sky
Though the miles stretch long and the nights feel cold
I'll keep this bar running till our story's told

The bottles clink loud but your voice is my sound
In this long distance mess where our hearts are bound
I'll wait behind the bar for the next call to ring
Punk love surviving on the hope it will bring

songs.subject: lo-fi / being misunderstood
songs.lyrics:
The kids on the porch they just laugh at my cane
Say I'm stuck in the past living life in the rain
But these wrinkles hold stories they never could know
Wars and lost loves that still steal the show
I tried to explain but the words come out wrong
They nod and they smile like I'm singing a song
That faded too soon on a radio wave
Now I'm left with the silence that nobody craves

Nobody gets me I'm just background noise
A record that skips on forgotten old joys
Nobody listens they think that I'm through
But the fire inside still burns clear and true

Grandkids visit and talk of their screens
I stare at the garden and think what it means
To live through the decades and watch the world change
Yet feel like a stranger in everything strange
The wife in the photos she knew every line
Now the house holds the echoes of a different time
I talk to the walls but they don't answer back
Just creak in the wind like they too feel the lack

Nobody gets me I'm just background noise
A record that skips on forgotten old joys
Nobody listens they think that I'm through
But the fire inside still burns clear and true

So I sit with my tea and the evening news drone
Wishing for once that my thoughts weren't alone
But the sun keeps on setting on this misunderstood man
And the world turns without ever quite understanding

songs.subject: pop / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
These streets still echo with the nights we walked
Hand in hand laughing while the old jukebox talked
Now the diner booth's empty and the neon looks dim
Every corner reminds me of what could have been
The park where we danced under summer moonlight
Now feels like a ghost town on a cold lonely night
I drive past our bench and the memories flood
This hometown's a heart that's been broken and bloodied

Hometown lights still shine but they don't feel the same
Every song on the radio calls out your name
Hometown streets keep turning but I'm lost in the rain
Dumped and alone in the place we once claimed

The coffee shop window shows tables for two
I order one cup like I always used to
The river we kissed by still flows to the sea
But it carries my heart farther from me
Friends say move on but the roots here run deep
And leaving this town feels like losing more sleep
I stare at the skyline we swore was our own
Now it's just silhouettes where your laughter has flown

Hometown lights still shine but they don't feel the same
Every song on the radio calls out your name
Hometown streets keep turning but I'm lost in the rain
Dumped and alone in the place we once claimed

Maybe tomorrow I'll pack and head west
But tonight every memory won't let me rest
This hometown's my prison my comfort my pain
Holding pieces of us that I'll never reclaim

songs.subject: bluegrass / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:

The radio crackles an old mountain tune
I pick up the banjo and we howl at the moon

Home with my family and that suits me fine
Neighbors bring cornbread and jars full of beans
We sit on the porch swapping stories and dreams
The war took some pieces but it left me my kin
That's richer than silver or gold in the bin
Broke as a joke but my soul feels fine
Home with my family and that suits me fine
Tomorrow might bring us another hard day
But tonight we got moonlight and nothing to pay
I hum to the crickets and hold my wife near
A soldier come home with his heart right here
Broke as a joke but my soul feels fine
Home with my family and that suits me fine

Another soul traveling under gray skies
I turn up my collar and head for the road

The blues in my chest gonna make grown men cry

songs.subject: lo-fi / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Wheels touch down and the engines grow still
Uniform pressed but my spirit feels ill
Gate number three where your eyes meet my own
One final moment before I am flown
Hand on the glass as the last call is made
Promises whispered in the plans that we laid
I'm saying goodbye at this cold airport door
Saying goodbye to the life we had before
Saying goodbye with a kiss that won't last
Hoping the future won't fade into past
Static on speakers and boarding in line
Your fingers slip slowly away from mine
Crowds push between us yet time stands so still
Carrying duty and this hollowed out will
Letters I'll write from some faraway base
Counting the days till I'm back in your space
I'm saying goodbye at this cold airport door
Saying goodbye to the life we had before

songs.lyrics:

Boot heels on gravel underneath every star
Serving cold longnecks to the same old crowd
Dreaming of gravel roads and the farm I left proud

Just open spaces and the peace that will last

Coming back home at the end of the day

Home in my bones and it feels mighty great

Coming back home where the mockingbirds sing
