songs.subject: blues / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
I heard the ring cut through the dark of night
Old wooden phone on the wall buzzing right
Whiskey on the table and my heart in my throat
Your voice came calling like a long lost ghost
Said you were sorry but the years slipped away
Now I'm sitting here wondering what to say

That midnight phone call it tears me apart
Brings back the rain and the ache in my heart
Midnight phone call can't turn it around
Leaves me here lonely with the same old sound

You spoke of the road and the miles you drove
How the blues took hold and you never let go
I listened in silence to the crack in your tone
Remembering the nights we called this our home
But the static was heavy and the line went dead
Left me with memories circling my head

That midnight phone call it tears me apart
Brings back the rain and the ache in my heart
Midnight phone call can't turn it around
Leaves me here lonely with the same old sound

Morning light creeps in but the feeling stays
Another day dawning in this same old haze
I pour another drink and I stare at the floor
Knowing deep down you'll call once more

Dear diary today the distance hits hard again
Texting from the station while I'm counting every mile
Work is steady but the nights feel cold without you near
Scrolling old photos wishing I could hold you for a while
Flights are too expensive and the time zones keep us split
But every late night call reminds me why we still commit

We chase the dream across the map with hearts on separate shores
Hoping one day soon we'll close this gap and open different doors
Diary pages fill with lines of love that won't let go
Long distance testing us but we refuse to fold

Dear diary the letters pile up in this empty room
Video calls glitch out but your smile still breaks through
Missed birthdays and holidays yet the bond stays true
Saving every penny till the day I'm back with you
Family asks when we'll settle but we both know the fight
Keeping faith alive through every lonely night

We chase the dream across the map with hearts on separate shores
Hoping one day soon we'll close this gap and open different doors
Diary pages fill with lines of love that won't let go
Long distance testing us but we refuse to fold

Dear diary the future's bright if we can just hold on
Counting down the moments till this chapter's finally done
Your voice on the line gives me strength to carry through
One day this distance will be nothing but a view

The phone lights up at the edge of my bed
Your name on the screen like a voice from the dead
I'm shaking I'm breaking I'm lost in the glow
Of words that we spoke years ago
You said you missed me but the silence grew
Now I'm bleeding these feelings all over you

I can't let go of this midnight call
It's pulling me under I'm ready to fall
Midnight phone call ripping me wide
All of these scars I still can't hide

Tears hit the pillow as memories flood
Of promises broken and rivers of blood
You whisper I'm sorry but it's already too late
I'm drowning in echoes of our twisted fate
The line goes quiet but the pain remains
Feeding the fire inside my veins

I can't let go of this midnight call
It's pulling me under I'm ready to fall
Midnight phone call ripping me wide
All of these scars I still can't hide

I stare at the ceiling the dark closing in
This endless cycle of where we begin
You hang up the phone but I still hear your name
Burning like embers inside the flame

Neon signs flicker on the old main street
I pushed your stroller through the summer heat
Watching the seasons turn the leaves to gold
While the factory whistle marked the days we hold
You learned to ride your bike on cracked sidewalks
Dreaming of highways and the city lights that talk
I worked two jobs just to keep the lights on
Praying you'd find more than these dusty dawns

I raised you here where the world moves slow
Hoping you'd grow but never let go
Small town roots and a mother's fight
Building your future in the pale moonlight

You'd sit on the porch counting stars at night
Telling me stories of taking flight
I smiled through the worry knowing one day
This little town would send you away
The carnival came and the fairgrounds glowed
You laughed with friends on the winding road
I watched from the sidelines my heart full of pride
A single mom cheering from the side

I raised you here where the world moves slow
Hoping you'd grow but never let go
Small town roots and a mother's fight
Building your future in the pale moonlight

Now the bags are packed and the bus pulls in
I wave from the corner with a trembling grin
This place made us strong in its quiet way
And I'll carry you with me every single day

songs.subject: R&B / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:
I'm done with the daily grind, pushing papers for a dime
Boss man yelling, clock watching, wasting all my time
Paycheck small, dreams are calling, gotta make a move
Pack my things, say goodbye, got nothing left to prove
I'm quitting my job today, walking out that door
No more chains on my soul, freedom I adore
Quitting my job today, chasing what is mine
Heart is light, future bright, this is my sign

I'm quitting my job today, walking out that door
No more chains on my soul, freedom I adore

Heart is light, future bright, this is my sign

Told my mama, told my brother, they said go and fly
Left the keys on the desk, never said goodbye
Now the road is open, singing loud and free
Quitting was the best thing that ever happened to me
I'm quitting my job today, walking out that door
No more chains on my soul, freedom I adore
Quitting my job today, chasing what is mine
Heart is light, future bright, this is my sign

That faded picture in the drawer, edges torn and worn
Your smile shining back at me, the day that I was born
For you, now it's just a memory of what we used to share
Dumped me like an old mistake, left me standing here
Oh that old photograph, brings the pain right back
Smiling faces lie to me, now everything is black
Old photograph, can't throw it away

Oh that old photograph, brings the pain right back

Photograph is all I have to keep me holding on
Oh that old photograph, brings the pain right back
Smiling faces lie to me, now everything is black
Old photograph, can't throw it away
Holds the love we lost today

songs.subject: gospel / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
I grew up on dusty roads where church bells rang at dawn
Mama's prayers and Sunday hymns kept my spirit strong
Small town streets where neighbors knew your every single name
Faith was planted in my soul before I knew the game
But Lord you carried me through valleys dark and wide
Now I'm standing here alone since love up and died
Raise me up on wings of grace, let the old town shine
Growing up again in faith, your light will make me fine

Played hide and seek by the riverbank, sang in the choir at night
Learned to trust in higher plans when everything felt right

songs.subject: ballad / a rainy Sunday
songs.lyrics:
The rain taps soft against the glass this Sunday morn
An old man sips his coffee cold and watches shadows form
The paper lies unopened like the years he left behind

Sunday lingers heavy in the silence of this chair
Time moves slower now with nothing left to do but wait

I hear her voice in every drop that traces down the pane
Reminding me of Sundays when the world was ours to claim
Kids would fill the hallway with their footsteps loud and bright
Now the house just echoes back the absence of their light

Rain keeps falling washing every dream we used to share
Sunday lingers heavy in the silence of this chair
Time moves slower now with nothing left to do but wait
For the clouds to pass and maybe ease this weary weight

Another hour passes and the light begins to fade
I fold the empty paper and I face the night ahead
Rain will come again tomorrow but the ache stays just the same
An old man and his memories on this quiet Sunday

songs.subject: bluegrass / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to a glow on the nightstand light
Phone screen shining like a cheap spotlight
Your words hit hard in that blue white glare
"Done with us" and you didn't even care
I read it twice with my heart in my throat
Empty bed and that old pickup note
You said it quick like a hit and run
Left me here with a screen and none

Don't text me now with your sorry line
That little screen just stole what was mine
I poured my soul in a message back
But the signal died on that lonesome track

Out in the holler where the signal fades
I stared at the words till my eyes got played
Mama always said don't trust a glow
Now I'm walking the ridge with a heavy load
Banjo strings hum in the morning chill
Thinking how quick you could type that kill
Rivers keep running but they don't reply
Just like that message that made me cry

Don't text me now with your sorry line
That little screen just stole what was mine
I poured my soul in a message back
But the signal died on that lonesome track

Tires keep turning on this endless black
Headlights cutting through the rain and lack
Radio static but I crank it high
Trying to outrun the way you said goodbye
Miles stack up like the thoughts in my head
Exit signs blur but I drive instead
Windows down letting the cold air bite

Your name still flashes on my phone
I hit decline and keep rolling on
Highway hum is the only friend
Curves and straightaways that never bend
I could turn around but I know I won't
This road's my way of staying afloat

songs.subject: emo / quitting your job

Clock on the wall ticks like a final call
Desk full of papers that meant nothing at all
Boss voice echoing in my tired mind
Forty years wasted in a suit and tie grind
Young ones laughing in the break room light
I sign the paper and I feel the fight
Leaving the keys on the old wood grain
Walking out slow through the pouring rain

No more alarms no more forced hello
Just the quiet ache of letting go
No more deadlines crushing down my soul
At the end of the line feeling whole and old

Porch light flickers as I sit alone
Pension check coming but the years have flown
Grandkids photos on the faded wall
Wondering if I gave it all for a stall
Coffee cooling in the morning chill
No boss to answer no more uphill
Regret and freedom in the same deep breath
Quitting too late but there's nothing left

No more alarms no more forced hello
Just the quiet ache of letting go
No more deadlines crushing down my soul
At the end of the line feeling whole and old

Learning how to truly feel alive

But they linger soft and bittersweet
In the quiet corners where we used to meet

I’m walking out the door with my apron on the floor

Teenage rebellion singing loud in the rain

I got playlists waiting and whole summer nights
No more rushing orders or smiling through the stress

Every shadow on the wall looks ready to fight

Insomnia’s got me pacing these cold concrete floors
Chasing silence that never opens its doors
Stars outside the window but peace stays away
Another long night turning minutes to days

Medics say rest but my mind won’t obey
Replaying the orders that sent friends away
I stare at the ceiling tracing cracks in the paint
Wondering if morning will ever feel safe

songs.subject: ballad / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Rent is late again and the fridge is half empty
But my little girl’s laughing while she colors with me
We dance in the kitchen to songs on the radio
Making castles from blankets when the lights are too low
Bills pile up like mountains but her smile keeps me warm
We got love in this tiny apartment that’s never for sale

Broke but happy that’s the life we chose
Counting blessings instead of what we don’t own
Her tiny hand in mine is richer than gold
We’re building our world with the little we hold

Neighbors bring casseroles when they know times are tight
We share stories on the stoop until stars fill the night
She asks if we’re rich and I tell her we’re free
As long as we’ve got each other that’s enough for me

Broke but happy that’s the life we chose
Counting blessings instead of what we don’t own
Her tiny hand in mine is richer than gold
We’re building our world with the little we hold

Heart racing faster than the beat in my veins
We talk about nothing but it feels like everything
Laughing in the corner while the speakers scream

I never believed in love at first sight until tonight
Now I’m falling hard in the glow of the strobe light
Emo kid with a crush that won’t let go
Hoping you feel the same before the night ends slow

Sparks like static on the crowded dance floor
We step outside where the air is cool and real

The city hums with electric beat

I wipe the counter and the same old scene

I slip on my heels and I head for the door

I'm free in the strobe lights forevermore

songs.subject: bluegrass / faith
songs.lyrics:
I sit in the pew with my doubts in my hands
Praying for answers in this shifting sand
The world pulls me sideways with every new trend
But I feel His whisper when the choir begins

Lord lift me up when my faith starts to fade
Guide these young steps on the path You have made
Through the storms and the questions You hold me so near
In Your endless mercy I cast out my fear

My friends chase the lights but I turn to the cross
Counting the blessings that never get lost
Homework and heartbreaks and dreams yet to come
I know You are with me till the battle is won

Lord lift me up when my faith starts to fade
Guide these young steps on the path You have made
Through the storms and the questions You hold me so near
In Your endless mercy I cast out my fear

Some days I stumble and wonder why pray
But Sunday mornings remind me to stay
The old hymns are singing deep down in my soul
Your love is the anchor that makes my heart whole

Lord lift me up when my faith starts to fade
Guide these young steps on the path You have made
Through the storms and the questions You hold me so near
In Your endless mercy I cast out my fear

songs.lyrics:
Headlights cut the darkness on this endless line

Another dawn rising but the shadows still come

songs.subject: alt rock / faith
songs.lyrics:
Waking up before the sun with two hearts to feed
Coffee in a chipped mug and a heart that still believes
The rent is overdue but I whisper through the fear
That the one who holds tomorrow sees each fallen tear
Laundry piles like mountains and the nights are long and cold
But I kneel beside their beds and let the stories unfold
Of a love that never leaves and a plan I can't yet see
Even when the world is crumbling faith is holding me

In the dark I still believe in the light I cannot see
Carrying these little lives he's walking here with me
Single mother tired and true but grace is what I find
Faith is all I have and faith is keeping us alive

School runs and late shifts and the weight upon my back
Praying for a miracle in every bill and crack
They ask me why I smile when the money's running thin
I tell them there's a father who has always let us in
Friends say give it up but my soul won't turn away
Every answered prayer is written in the gray
I hold them close and sing the hymns my mother used to know
And watch the smallest blessings make the broken places glow

In the dark I still believe in the light I cannot see
Carrying these little lives he's walking here with me
Single mother tired and true but grace is what I find
Faith is all I have and faith is keeping us alive

Years will come and questions rise about the road we've walked
But every tear I've cried has turned into a talk
With the one who never fails even when I doubt
Faith is not a feeling it's the rope that pulls me out

songs.subject: blues / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
Empty pockets in my jeans but the summer air is free
Riding bikes down empty streets with my friends and me
No cash for movies but the radio is loud
We laugh until the stars come out and time just slips around
Mom says save for college but the present feels so right
A dollar in my sock and dreams that fill the night
Broke as hell but happy in the way the wind feels clean
Living for the little things that money's never seen

Ain't got much but I got enough to smile
Broke and young and running wild for a while
Happiness don't check my wallet it just shows
In the songs we sing and the places that we go

Skip the mall and head to parks where no one needs a fee
Trading stories on the swings like they were currency
No new shoes but these old ones still know every crack
We chase the sun until it sets and never once look back
Homework waits but so does life and right now life is mine
A few coins for a soda and the taste is just fine
They say get a job but I'm still learning how to be
Happy in the moment even when it's only me

Ain't got much but I got enough to smile
Broke and young and running wild for a while
Happiness don't check my wallet it just shows
In the songs we sing and the places that we go

One day I'll be older with bills I can't ignore
But tonight the world's still open like an unlocked door
Broke but laughing louder than the troubles on my mind
This kind of happy money never seems to find

songs.subject: hip-hop / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Used to ride the bus together splitting headphones in the rain
Now your side is empty and the silence feels the same
Scrolling through old pictures on my phone late at night
Wondering if you still remember how we owned the daylight
You packed up in September for a city far away
Left me with these memories that never seem to fade
We said we'd text forever but the days are getting long
Missing how we used to finish every other's song

You moved away and took a piece I didn't know I'd need
Now I'm here repeating every word you used to read
Teenage nights feel heavier without you in the mix
Wishing on the same old stars for one more night like this

School halls echo different when your laugh is out of reach
I pass the spots we claimed and practice how to teach
Myself to keep on going like you told me I could do
But every corner holds a ghost of me and you
New friends try to fill the space but they don't know the code
The inside jokes we carried on the long way home from school
I save your voicemails just to hear you say my name
Hoping that the distance someday won't feel quite the same

You moved away and took a piece I didn't know I'd need
Now I'm here repeating every word you used to read
Teenage nights feel heavier without you in the mix
Wishing on the same old stars for one more night like this

Maybe when the summer comes I'll save enough to ride
Out to where you landed and see the other side
Until then I'm counting down the messages you send
Holding on to everything until we meet again

songs.subject: disco / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
Boot steps on the floorboards and the bottle's in the sink
I traded nights of numbness for the will to never drink
The mission changed when morning came without the taste of doubt
Now I'm dancing to the rhythm of a life I'm figuring out
Lights are flashing colors like the ones inside my head
Sober steps are steady even when the past feels dead
From the barracks to the dance floor I am learning how to feel
Every beat is freedom from the things I used to steal

I'm getting sober under disco lights so bright
Turning down the bottle for a brand new kind of fight
Soldier heart is beating to a sober kind of sound
Finally alive and spinning when the music comes around

Sweat on my forehead but it's clean and it is mine
No more hiding in the whiskey when the memories unwind
Brothers back at base might still be pouring out the pain
But I'm here choosing movement over letting it remain
The mirror in the bathroom shows a face I recognize
Eyes are clear and open underneath the spinning lights
Every chord is lifting what the war tried to drag down
I'm sober on the floor and wearing freedom like a crown

I'm getting sober under disco lights so bright
Turning down the bottle for a brand new kind of fight
Soldier heart is beating to a sober kind of sound
Finally alive and spinning when the music comes around

Days will come with triggers but the groove will pull me through
I fought for this tomorrow and tomorrow I will choose
To keep the glass empty and the spirit open wide
Dancing into morning with my sober heart alive

songs.subject: blues / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Woke up to the glow of my phone in the dark
Your words lit up like a brand new scar
Said you found someone who makes you feel right
Left me staring at the screen all night
My heart's in pieces on the kitchen floor
Can't believe it's over nothing more
That little blue bubble changed everything
Now the rain outside just starts to sing

That breakup text came through like thunder
Turned my whole world into blues and wonder
Said goodbye in a line or two
Left me here with nothing but you

I poured a drink but it tasted like pain
Thought about calling but what's the use in vain
We had plans for the weekend and forever
Now it's just me and this stormy weather
Your name in my contacts feels like a lie
I keep reading it over wondering why
The silence after your message is loud
Got me chained to these memories now

That breakup text came through like thunder
Turned my whole world into blues and wonder
Said goodbye in a line or two
Left me here with nothing but you

Woke up again and the sun feels cold
This old guitar is the only one I hold
Singing these lines till the morning light
Hoping someday this pain takes flight

songs.subject: alt rock / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
The final bell rang and the halls went wild
Kids spilling out with that reckless smile
I wiped down the bar where they used to sneak in
Pretending to be older than the state they're in
Another round of shots for the ones who made it
While I'm stuck here pouring for the ones who fake it
Last day of school and the summer calls
But my shift ends when the last one falls

These empty glasses tell the same old tale
Of dreams that fade when the young ones bail
I'm raising a glass to the ones who leave
While the jukebox spins what I can't believe

The parking lot cleared like a ghost town now
Left me with bottles and a furrowed brow
They'll chase the highways and forget this place
While I'm here mixing drinks at a slower pace
One kid said thanks for the wisdom I gave
But I just nodded like a working slave
The lights dim down on this worn-out stage
Last day of school and I'm twice their age

These empty glasses tell the same old tale
Of dreams that fade when the young ones bail
I'm raising a glass to the ones who leave
While the jukebox spins what I can't believe

Tomorrow comes and the kids are gone
I'm left with the keys and another dawn

songs.subject: country / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Dirt roads wind through the fields I know

The diner booth where we planned it all

Old friends wave but their eyes look away
Knowing the news of our final day
Barn dances echo with songs we danced
Now the fiddles cry while my heart's entranced
By the fields that stretch to a future alone
In this place that still feels like home

Hometown lights keep shining on
Even when your love is gone
These streets hold the pieces of me
Trying to forget what we used to be

Sunset falls on the water tower sign
Reminding me your heart was never mine

You make the static fade away

Falling slow like the vinyl spin

You make the static fade away

Maybe love can find me even when I'm this deep

songs.subject: americana / a funeral
songs.lyrics:
The pews are filled with folks who knew his name
I write it down so I don't forget the way
The flowers on the casket smell like rain
His old boots by the door will stay the same
I sip the coffee black just like he did
And wonder how the years all slipped away

Dear diary the preacher spoke his name
The dirt was cold the sky was turning gray
I held the folded flag against my chest
And tried to find the words he would have said
The road goes on but I'm still here instead

The neighbors brought a pie and stayed a while
We told the stories that could make us smile
His chair sits empty at the kitchen table
The quiet fills the room like it is able
I trace the lines he carved into the wood
And pray the ache will soften like it should

Dear diary the preacher spoke his name
The dirt was cold the sky was turning gray
I held the folded flag against my chest
And tried to find the words he would have said
The road goes on but I'm still here instead

The sun went down behind the old oak tree
I closed the book and let the evening be

songs.subject: hip-hop / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:

Bikes on gravel roads and the county fair lights chasing dreams under small town skies
Mama worked two jobs dad drank away the pain we hid our scars in the pouring rain
Football on Fridays church on Sunday mornings but the world outside kept calling my name
Small town chains they hold you tight soldier boots marching into the fight
Small town fires burning bright but I left it all for a different life
Small town chains they hold you tight soldier boots marching into the night

Came back a man with medals on my chest but the town looked smaller than I could have guessed
Friends in the ground or stuck in the same mess while I'm haunted by ghosts in my army vest
Small town chains they hold you tight soldier boots marching into the fight
Small town fires burning bright but I left it all for a different life
Small town chains they hold you tight soldier boots marching into the night
Small town fires burning bright but I left it all for a different life
Now I walk these streets with a limp in my stride carrying the weight of a world outside
Small town boy with a soldier's pride but the chains still rattle deep down inside

songs.lyrics:
The lilies on the table smelled like rain that never fell

songs.subject: lo-fi / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:

Laughing at nothing just me and you

It's the last day of school and the house feels too still
I'm proud of my boy but the quiet hits hard

Watching him walk away gets a little bit harder

Tonight I'll sit out on the back step alone

Hoping the road ahead won't feel so long
Knowing he'll be okay even when I'm gone

songs.lyrics:
Woke up with the morning light painting the old red barn
Tractors humming down the dirt road like a familiar song
Mama's kitchen smells like coffee and biscuits rising high
Neighbors waving from porches as the years go drifting by
Fields stretch out forever where we chased the fireflies at night

Sunsets paint the horizon in those colors only we know

Every goodbye is a promise that this land will never let go
Potholes on the main street mark the paths we walked before
Church bells ring on Sunday calling us to something more
Memories like river stones smoothed by time's steady flow
In this hometown every chapter starts and ends with what we know
Oh my hometown you call me home no matter where I roam

songs.subject: blues / an ex you still think about
songs.lyrics:
Porch boards creak beneath these boots that walked a thousand miles with her
the sun is sinking slow and red just like it always did back then
her photograph is faded now but I still see the smile she gave
when we were young and foolish sure the world would never end
now my hands shake pouring whiskey that can’t drown the quiet nights
and every song on the old radio still carries bits of her

She’s still walking through my mind at eighty-two
She’s still walking through my mind at eighty-two

The kids don’t visit much these days they say I talk too much of her
the garden that we planted once has gone to weeds and time
I tell the mirror every morning that I’m finally letting go
but then the evening comes and brings her laughter back again
my cane is propped against the chair where she would sit and read
and every breath I take reminds me how the story should have been

She’s still walking through my mind at eighty-two

Neon pulses through the wires in my chest again tonight
your signal flickers on the screen I swore I’d turn to black
synthesizers in the dark replay the words we used to send
I press repeat and let the static fill the empty room
your name still lights the panel when the algorithm dreams
and every beat drops like the moment you walked out the door

You’re the frequency I can’t delete
You’re the frequency I can’t delete

Circuit boards remember every touch we mapped in code
the city glows below my window but it feels so far away
I filter every memory through layers of delay and gain
your voice still echoes in the mix when silence starts to creep
I dance alone beneath the lasers that used to feel like home
and chase the phantom of your heartbeat through the endless loop

songs.subject: synthpop / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
Sunset on the playground swings are slowing down
Little feet in sandals kicking at the ground
I fold the beach towels one last time this year
Summer slips away like it was never really here

Back to school lists and early alarm clocks
Trading lazy mornings for the ticking of the clock
I watch them laugh and wonder where the days have flown
Single mom horizon and I'm standing here alone

Summer's almost over now the breeze is turning cool
Summer's almost over but the memories still rule
Holding onto every hour every wave that rolled on by
Watching summer fade beneath a pink and orange sky

They ask me for ice cream one more time before it's gone
I say yes and take a picture so the moment carries on
Work is calling Monday and the rent is due next week
But tonight we chase the fireflies before the season peaks

Summer's almost over now the breeze is turning cool
Summer's almost over but the memories still rule
Holding onto every hour every wave that rolled on by
Watching summer fade beneath a pink and orange sky

They'll remember this when they're older I hope they do
I close my eyes and breathe it in the way that mothers do

songs.subject: emo / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
Lockers slamming echoes down the empty hall
Last bell ringing like it's saying that's all
Your locker next to mine but you're already gone
I linger by the window as the final students run

We carved our names in the desk last spring
Now the wood feels colder than anything
I wanted to tell you before the year went dark
How every hallway moment left a permanent mark

The last day of school and I'm standing here alone
The last day of school with a heart turning to stone
You smiled at me once like the world could wait
Now the summer stretches and I can't escape

I walk the empty courtyard where we used to meet
Graffiti on the wall still says your favorite beat
My backpack feels heavier with words I never said
The bus pulls away and leaves me here instead

The last day of school and I'm standing here alone
The last day of school with a heart turning to stone
You smiled at me once like the world could wait
Now the summer stretches and I can't escape

Maybe next September you'll remember my name
But tonight the emo sky is starting to rain

Empty drawers and a phone that won't ring

songs.subject: rock / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
Neon signs are buzzing like a secret code
Skateboard wheels on wet concrete echo down the road
Storefronts dark except the corner store light
I'm out past curfew chasing the night
Sirens in the distance but they ain't for me
City's breathing slow at a quarter to three

Three a.m. and the streets are mine to own
Headphones loud and the world feels overgrown
No one asking where I'm going or why
Three a.m. feels like the only time I'm alive

Subway rumbles under my feet like thunder
Graffiti walls tell stories I still wonder
Coffee steam from a vendor who don't ask my name
I could stay out here forever in the rain
Phone's on silent 'cause home don't need to know
I'm chasing freedom where the streetlights glow

Three a.m. and the streets are mine to own
Headphones loud and the world feels overgrown

Tomorrow's just a rumor I can outrun
Till the sun comes up and the spell is done

Three a.m. and the streets are mine to own
Headphones loud and the world feels overgrown
No one asking where I'm going or why
Three a.m. feels like the only time I'm alive

songs.subject: rock / a long-distance relationship
songs.lyrics:
The baby's asleep but your voice is on the line
Miles between us stretching out like time
I tell you about her first steps on the phone
You say you're coming soon but we both know
Work keeps you there and the bills keep me here
Love stretched thin but it still feels near

Long distance love in the quiet of the night
Holding on to every word until the light
Single mom counting days till you come home
Long distance love but we're never alone

She draws your picture with crayons on the floor
Asks when daddy's back like she did before
I smile through the ache and change the subject fast
Promise her stories while the months go past
Your letters come stained from the road and the rain
I read them twice to ease the lonely pain

Long distance love in the quiet of the night
Holding on to every word until the light
Single mom counting days till you come home
Long distance love but we're never alone

The kitchen's quiet now the dishes are done
I whisper your name to the setting sun
We make it work with calls and dreams we share
Even when the distance feels too hard to bear

Long distance love but we're never alone

Rainy Sunday setting my old heart at ease

Blanket on the couch where the kids used to play
Letters from the drawer yellowed at the fold

Thunder rolls in soft like laughter from the past
Holding on to moments that were never meant to last

songs.subject: country / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
Dear diary today the rain fell hard on the old front porch swing
I reached for the phone but there's no one left to hear me sing
Your boots still sit by the door like they might walk back in someday
The fields we plowed together now just fade to shades of gray
The radio plays that old song you hummed when the work was through
I miss your steady hand on my shoulder guiding me true
Dear diary the supper table's set for one and it don't feel right
Your chair stays empty in the corner under the pale porch light
We laughed about the old times chasing dreams down the dirt road bend
Now every memory stings like whiskey at the bitter end
The radio plays that old song you hummed when the work was through
I miss your steady hand on my shoulder guiding me true
Dear diary I found your letters tucked away in the barn last night
Your words still echo in my heart keeping the dark at bay with light
I'll keep your lessons close and carry on the best I can
Till the day we meet again on that far celestial land
The radio plays that old song you hummed when the work was through
I miss your steady hand on my shoulder guiding me true

songs.subject: bluegrass / insomnia
songs.lyrics:
The clock strikes midnight and these old bones won't settle down
I stare at the ceiling counting cracks in this worn-out town
The wind outside whistles like a fiddle in the square
But sleep won't come no matter how I rock this rocking chair
Been seventy years since the war and these nights drag on slow
Tossing turning listening to the hours come and go
The fire burns low but my mind keeps racing back
Through the faces and the places down a long and winding track
The moon hangs heavy and the crickets sing their tune
Yet my eyes stay open chasing shadows in the room
Been seventy years since the war and these nights drag on slow
Tossing turning listening to the hours come and go
The porch light flickers as the dawn begins to break
Another restless evening leaves my weary spirit ache
I brew the coffee strong and watch the world come alive
Wondering if tonight the same old story will arrive
The moon hangs heavy and the crickets sing their tune
Yet my eyes stay open chasing shadows in the room

songs.subject: rock / a breakup text
songs.lyrics:
Dear diary the screen lit up and then it all went black
Her words hit like a freight train no turning coming back
She said it's over sorry but I'm moving on from here
I stared at the message till the letters blurred with tears
The band was blasting loud but silence filled my head
I typed a hundred answers that I never sent instead
Dear diary the phone still sits there on the table cold
Her name is gone from favorites like a story left untold
We danced through summer nights now the music's turned to noise
I replay every promise breaking all my foolish toys
The band was blasting loud but silence filled my head
I typed a hundred answers that I never sent instead
Dear diary I walked the streets till morning touched the sky
Her text is burned forever but I'll learn to say goodbye
The stage calls out tomorrow and I'll shout it from the mic
Yet every chord reminds me of that final final strike
The band was blasting loud but silence filled my head
I typed a hundred answers that I never sent instead

Now they steady on the morning grass

Old man walking in a brand new phase
Voices from the past start to fade away
Sober light shows me a brighter way

I sit on the porch where the bottles used to stack

Sober light breaks through the haze

Sober light shows me a brighter way

Memories linger like clouds in the sky

songs.lyrics:
Tiny fingers curl around my thumb
A whole new world has just begun
Soft cries echo in the quiet room
Filling the space where the old days loom
Eyes like stars in the dim bedside light
Everything changes with you here tonight

You are the promise I never knew
A fragile dream that feels brand new
Hold you close and the world stands still
Love like this could heal any ill

Diapers and bottles line the shelf
I'm learning to be someone else
Your first smile cracks the morning gray
Chasing the fears that used to stay
Tiny socks scattered across the floor
Each one a step toward something more

You are the promise I never knew
A fragile dream that feels brand new
Hold you close and the world stands still

Promises whispered while you sleep
Future unwinds with each little breath

songs.lyrics:

Windows down and the past blows free
Just me and the highway and what used to be

Radio crackles an old country tune
Brings back the summers that ended too soon
Hand on the wheel feels steady and right
Chasing the daylight into the night
Kids all grown and the house too still
But the road keeps rolling with its own will

Windows down and the past blows free
Just me and the highway and what used to be
No need to hurry no place to land
Driving these roads with these worn out hands

Truck stop coffee and a smile from the past

Another mile closer to the end of the day

songs.subject: punk / an ex you still think about
songs.lyrics:
Woke up at dawn with your name on my tongue
Screaming at walls but the damage is done
We burned it all down in a fit of rage
Now I'm picking through ashes on a minimum wage
You said we were poison but I liked the taste
Chasing your ghost through this dump of a place
I told all my friends that I'm better off free
But the truth is your shadow still hangs over me

I still think about you when the bass gets loud
I still think about you in this pissed-off crowd
Can't kill the memory no matter how hard I try
You're the itch I can't scratch and I don't know why

Threw your old records straight into the street
Watched the tires crush every lie and repeat
Got new tattoos just to cover your name
But the ink won't forget how we went down in flames
I party till sunrise and wake up alone
Swearing this time that your memory is gone
Yet every damn stranger still looks like your eyes
And I'm stuck in this loop of bullshit goodbyes

I still think about you when the bass gets loud
I still think about you in this pissed-off crowd
Can't kill the memory no matter how hard I try
You're the itch I can't scratch and I don't know why

Another night shift pouring whiskey for ghosts
Same old faces but none of them close
Told the boss I'm leaving this dead-end town
Packed my bags while the neon burned down
Heading out west where the skyline is strange
Chasing new voices to drown out the change
These bottles I serve hold the stories I leave
Every pour is a promise I won't stay to grieve

I still think about you when the bass gets loud
I still think about you in this pissed-off crowd
Can't kill the memory no matter how hard I try
You're the itch I can't scratch and I don't know why

songs.subject: rock / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
Dust on the highway and the rearview is clean
Left the old apartment with the rent still green
Found a bar on the corner with a view of the trains
Mixing drinks for strangers while I forget your name
The taps run steady but the nights feel new
Every face at the rail is a story breaking through
I used to pour your favorite just to feel you near
Now I'm learning the locals and the price of a beer

Wheels keep turning on this borrowed time
Serving up tomorrow in a city that isn't mine
The bottles clink louder than the voice in my head
Telling me I'm better off than the words we never said

Late shift ends when the streetlights blur
Counting tips and wondering what I left her
A postcard in my pocket from the place I fled
Postmarked with the silence of the things unsaid
The jukebox plays songs that I used to know
But the melodies twist in the neon glow
I smile at the customers and hide the ache
Building a new life with every shift I take

songs.subject: indie folk / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
The porch light flickers where we used to sit
Your suitcase wheels fading down the gravel split
I stood in the doorway with nothing to say
Just watched the taillights carry you away
The fields stretch empty under the same old sky
Every bird that passes makes me wonder why
You chose the distance over what we had grown
And left me here counting the nights alone

The letters I write never make it to the mail
Just sit on the table growing yellow and pale
I walk the old path through the tall grass and weeds
Hearing your laugh in the rustle of leaves

The radio hums but the songs all sound wrong
Your favorite melody drags the silence along
I keep the light on though I know you're not near
Hoping the dark will bring you back here

Just sit on the table growing yellow and pale
I walk the old path through the tall grass and weeds

Watch the regulars laugh while my phone stays froze

Now the barstool’s empty when my shift is through
Rushing home to bottles that ain’t the ones I knew
Tips in my pocket but the real payoff’s there

Shaking up a new life behind the counter lights

Telling them my world just got a brand new address
The jukebox plays the same but the soundtrack’s rearranged
From closing tabs to hearing how her first word’s arranged
Still pouring for the lonely but my heart’s on hold
For the little one waiting when the night gets old

songs.subject: rock / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
The box was dusty in the attic where the shadows stay
I opened up the lid and let the memories replay
Black and white and faded but the faces still were mine
Young man with a future and a girl I thought was fine
We stood beside the old oak with the wind in our hair
Promises we carved there that the years would never tear
Now my hands are slower and the mirror tells the truth
That photograph is louder than the silence of my youth

Those days are gone but the paper still holds on
One frozen moment when the light was warm
I trace the edges gently like I’m touching who I was
An old man with the past inside a simple paper clause

We laughed about tomorrow like it owed us everything
Built a life on hope and all the songs we used to sing
The camera caught the promise before the letters stopped
Before the house grew quiet and the clocks began to drop
I keep it in the drawer now where the light won’t fade it more
But sometimes in the evening I still open up that door
The smile in the picture doesn’t know what’s coming next
Yet I hold it like a compass when my days are getting vexed

Those days are gone but the paper still holds on
One frozen moment when the light was warm
I trace the edges gently like I’m touching who I was
An old man with the past inside a simple paper clause

songs.subject: pop / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
You said you needed space and I gave you all I had
Drove the kids to practice while you chased another plan
Empty seat at dinner turned to months without a call
I learned to braid her hair and fix the cracks inside these walls
Forgiveness isn’t easy when the bills stack high alone
But I’m teaching them to stand when every piece of me has flown
Late nights folding laundry with the radio turned low
I whisper to the ceiling that I’m learning how to let it go

I forgive you for the leaving though the ache still lingers here
I forgive you for the silence when the future felt unclear
Not because you asked it but because my heart needs rest
So the ones who still need me see a mother who is blessed

They ask about their father and I answer with the truth
That love can change its address yet still carry weight and proof
I pay the rent on time now and the garden’s growing green
Found strength inside the pieces that you never thought I’d need
Some nights the anger rises but I let it pass on through
Because the kids deserve a version of me that’s whole and new
I sign the papers cleanly with a hand that doesn’t shake
And tell myself tomorrow is a chance I get to take

I forgive you for the leaving though the ache still lingers here
I forgive you for the silence when the future felt unclear
Not because you asked it but because my heart needs rest
So the ones who still need me see a mother who is blessed

songs.subject: country / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Suitcase by the gate and the boarding call is near
I wrote this in my notebook so the words would stay right here
Your hand was cold this morning when we said we’d try again
But the plane won’t wait for promises we’re not ready to defend
I kissed you at security with a smile that didn’t reach
Watched you disappear down the hallway like a lesson I can’t teach
The diary page is open and the ink is running slow
Telling all the reasons why it’s time for me to go

Airport lights are fading as the wheels begin to turn
I’m leaving half my heart in the seat beside where you still burn
Pages filled with airport coffee and the words I couldn’t say
Hoping distance makes the goodbye softer than it felt today

The terminal is quiet now except the echo in my chest
Every line I write is trying to explain the emptiness
You waved from the window like the future wasn’t planned
I closed the book and whispered that I’ll miss the life we had
The diary stays beside me on the seat that’s flying east
Filled with all the reasons this departure feels like peace
Yet every word still carries how your laughter used to sound
Before the gate closed softly on the love we both unwound

Airport lights are fading as the wheels begin to turn
I’m leaving half my heart in the seat beside where you still burn
Pages filled with airport coffee and the words I couldn’t say
Hoping distance makes the goodbye softer than it felt today

songs.subject: trap / quitting your job
songs.lyrics:

Bills piled up and the rent is due now

I'm walking out today no looking back
Stacking for my babies on a brand new track
Trap queen rising from a dead-end trap

Kids in the back and the future's mine

Building an empire from the seeds I sow
I'm walking out today no looking back
Stacking for my babies on a brand new track
Trap queen rising from a dead-end trap
Quitting this job for the life I deserve
No more nine to five I'ma make it work
Kids in the back and the future's mine

Home to the wife with the same old past

Faith lifts me when I start to fall

To the one who hears when the world is asleep
Promises kept while the shadows creep

Hallelujah in the midnight call
Faith lifts me when I start to fall
You answered before I spoke a word
Grace flowing down like I've never heard

Now the line is quiet but my soul sings on
Diary of mercy from dusk till dawn

songs.lyrics:
I close the taps when the last fool staggers out
The neon flickers and the jukebox dies
Keys in my pocket and the gravel calls me home
Headlights cutting through the same old lies
Empty bottles rattle in the passenger seat
Radio preaching what I already know
Every mile marker laughs at the plans I made
But the engine hums and I just let it go

This road keeps pulling like a rope around my chest
Takes me nowhere but it won't let me rest
Bartender's blues on a two-lane line
One more sunrise that ain't really mine

The regulars all have their stories they repeat
I nod along but I'm already gone
Past the old mill where the river used to run
Past the turnoff where I swore I'd stay
My hands are steady but my mind still shakes
From the things I poured and the tips I saved
The dashboard clock says it's half past gone
And the yellow lines keep counting down the days

This road keeps pulling like a rope around my chest
Takes me nowhere but it won't let me rest
Bartender's blues on a two-lane line
One more sunrise that ain't really mine

songs.subject: alt rock / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
The wallpaper peeled where we used to mark heights
Crayon lines fading like the years we spent
Porch light busted but the swing still creaks
Ghost of a ball bouncing down the hall again
Mom's old radio sits dusty on the shelf
Tuned to stations that no longer exist
Front door sticks the way it always did
Letting in the cold and the things we missed

Boards remember every footstep that ran
Windows hold the faces that are gone
I push the door and the past comes rushing in
Same old smell of rain on the wooden floor

Dad's toolbox rusts in the basement light
Sister's posters still hang on the wall
I walk the rooms and the silence answers back
Telling me the home was never really small
Now it's empty but the echoes fill the space
Every laugh and fight still trapped inside
I close the door and the lock clicks soft
Leaving all the living that we tried

songs.subject: punk / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
Lockers slammed for the final time today
Bells are dead and the halls are ours to break
Teachers packing up their rules and grades
We torch the notebooks in the parking lot flames
Caps in the air like we just declared war
On every early morning and the tests we failed
Backpacks empty but our heads are full of now
No more detentions and no more tales

Last day of school and the gates swing wide
Freedom screaming from the passenger side
Burn the books and the uniforms too
Nothing left to hold us back from what we do

The principal's voice crackles on the old PA
Telling us to behave but we're already gone
We pile in cars with the radio cranked high
Singing every song that we were told was wrong
Diplomas rolled like flags we wave goodbye
To the cafeteria food and the morning drills
One last cheer for the years we survived
Then we peel out spinning tires on the hill

Last day of school and the gates swing wide
Freedom screaming from the passenger side
Burn the books and the uniforms too
Nothing left to hold us back from what we do

songs.lyrics:

Can't sleep when the bottles whisper all the stories left untold

Wiped down every table but the stains won't fade away

songs.subject: americana / forgiveness
songs.lyrics:
I walked through fire in a land that took my youth
Bullets wrote their stories on the skin I call my own
Came back to faces that had changed beyond the truth
Of letters sent from trenches where the seeds of hate were sown
Forgive the hands that pulled the trigger in the rain
Forgive the silence that grew wider every mile
I lay my weapon down and wash away the stain
In rivers running deep beneath the southern style
The road back home was paved with every ghost I knew
A uniform can hide the heart but not the weight it bears
I kneel beside the graves and whisper what is true
That mercy finds its way through all the thorns and prayers
I see the enemy in dreams that never fade
Yet in the light of dawn I choose to let it go
Forgiveness is the flag that waves above the blade
A softer anthem rising from the fields below
Forgive the hands that pulled the trigger in the rain
Forgive the silence that grew wider every mile
I lay my weapon down and wash away the stain
In rivers running deep beneath the southern style
The children play where once the cannons roared so loud

A soldier's final battle is the one inside the shroud
Where love redeems the broken and the stories told

songs.subject: americana / working a dead-end job
songs.lyrics:
I wake before the rooster with the coffee burning black
My boots are scuffed and heavy on this same familiar track
The factory whistle cuts the air like it did ten years ago
I clock in with the others where the hours drag so slow
The bossman says we’re family but the raises never come
I’m stacking boxes higher till my back is feeling numb
Friday brings a paycheck that won’t stretch to next week’s rent
I’m living for the weekend that gets smaller as it’s spent

Another day another dollar in this town that won’t let go
Another year of wondering where the good years had to go
I keep punching in and hoping but the light stays out of reach
Dead-end road keeps winding with no exit left to teach

My buddy got a better job and moved on down the line
He sends a postcard every Christmas with a smile that isn’t mine
The kids are growing up and asking why we never take a trip
I tell them stories ’bout the future while I tighten up my grip
The radio plays old anthems from when dreams were still alive
I hum along in traffic where the taillights come in fives
One more shift till Sunday when the lawn needs mowing done
I wonder if the sun will set before my race is run

Another day another dollar in this town that won’t let go
Another year of wondering where the good years had to go
I keep punching in and hoping but the light stays out of reach
Dead-end road keeps winding with no exit left to teach

But the road still pulled me onward no matter where I’d roam
One night under starlight I heard my own song play
And knew the chase was worth it though the price I had to pay

songs.subject: dream pop / a childhood home
songs.lyrics:
The wallpaper in the hallway still curls at the corner seam
I wrote my name there once in crayon when I was turning seven
The porch swing creaks the same way when the wind comes through the screen
And the kitchen light flickers like it did when we were leaving
Mom’s old radio sits quiet now on the counter by the door
I open drawers and find the letters I was never meant to read
The floorboards hold the footprints from a thousand games of tag
I sit on the bottom stair and listen to the silence breathe

This house remembers everything I tried so hard to hide
The summer storms the secrets and the nights I stayed inside
Pages turn like windows in a diary left behind
Every room is writing what I’m still too scared to find

The attic holds the boxes with my first pair of ballet shoes
Dust settles on the curtains where the sunlight used to land
I trace the height marks on the doorframe with a fingertip that shakes
And hear my younger laughter echo from the other end of the hall
The garden out the window has grown wild without her hands
Roses climb the trellis like they’re reaching for the sky
I close the front door softly like I’m leaving part of me
But the diary keeps turning every time I say goodbye

songs.subject: ballad / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The kettle’s on the stove and the evening news is low
I move across the linoleum in shoes I used to know
The record player hums an old tune from our courting days
I take her hand in memory and let the music play
The chairs are pushed against the wall the table cleared of mail
I turn in slow half circles where her laughter used to sail
The window shows the streetlight and the years upon my face
I dance alone in rhythm with the ghost of her embrace

The clock ticks on the mantle like it always did at nine
I keep the steps we practiced when her hand was warm in mine
The radio fades to static but the melody stays on
I waltz across the quiet till the last of daylight’s gone

The fridge light flickers open when I reach for something cold
I close it with a soft click and return to what I hold
The floor remembers every turn we made on Friday nights
I dance a little longer in the circle of the light
Her perfume seems to linger in the air above the sink
I hum the words she whispered when we didn’t need to think
The song is almost over but I bow and start again
An old man in the kitchen dancing with what might have been

songs.subject: country / being misunderstood
songs.lyrics:
I've been that quiet kid in the back of the class
Folks shake their heads when I don't move too fast
They say I'm lazy but they don't see the fight
I'm just holding my ground in the dark of the night
They talk about me like they know what I've seen
But the truth's in the silence and the space in between
I walk these old roads with my head held low
Trying to find somewhere they might let me go

Why can't they see the man that I am
Why do they judge by the lines in my hands
I'm more than the rumors and the stories they tell
Just trying to be heard in a world raising hell
Misunderstood but I'm standing here still
With a heart full of fire and an ironclad will

Mama says pray but the answers don't come
Daddy just nods like he's deaf and he's dumb
Neighbors all whisper when I pass by their door
They don't know the battles I've already fought for
I write in my notebook the things I can't say
Hoping one day they'll all see it my way
The wind in the pines sings a lonesome tune
Just like my heart underneath this old moon

Why can't they see the man that I am
Why do they judge by the lines in my hands
I'm more than the rumors and the stories they tell
Just trying to be heard in a world raising hell
Misunderstood but I'm standing here still
With a heart full of fire and an ironclad will

One day I'll leave and they won't understand
But the road will be mine and I'll make my own stand

Trains rumble distant through the tunnels below
Lovers in doorways with nowhere to go
Coffee cups cold on the corner cafe
Bartenders wiping the last of the day
I see your face in every window shine
But the city keeps moving and leaves me behind
Wishing on streetlights that burn through the haze
Hoping the morning will soften the days

Three in the morning with tears in my eye
Three in the morning where the lost ones roam
Three in the morning and I'm all alone

The letter came stained from the desert sun
Your words like home when the fighting is done
Miles stretch forever across this wire
I hold your picture and feel the fire
Boots in the sand and your voice in my head
Telling me soon we'll be sharing our bed
The radio crackles but it can't replace
The feel of your hand and your warm embrace

Darling hold on through the miles and the rain
I'm counting the days till I'm home again
Our love's like the highway it bends and it turns
But every mile brings me closer to you

I write in the dark with a lantern low
Telling you stories only soldiers know
The ache in my chest when the wind blows cold
Thinking of you and the life we hold
Your last photo's faded from the sweat and the dust
But your smile still lifts me when the road feels rough
We talk on the phone when the lines allow
But nothing compares to your arms around me now

Darling hold on through the miles and the rain
I'm counting the days till I'm home again
Our love's like the highway it bends and it turns
But every mile brings me closer to you

When this tour ends I'll be on that plane
Racing the sunset to kiss you again

songs.subject: punk / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
You packed up your records and you caught that train
Left me with nothing but a hole and some rain
The neighborhood's quiet like it lost its spark
I kick at the curb in the growing dark
Your jacket still hangs on the back of my door
Smells like your perfume and the nights we ignored
I blast the old stereo till the neighbors complain
But nothing drowns out that you're gone again

I miss you like crazy in this empty town
The walls keep repeating the words that went down
Punk rock and heartache on the same damn track
I want you back now and I ain't looking back

The bar down the street plays our favorite song
I stand in the corner pretending you're not gone
Your friends all ask me if I'm doing okay
I lie through my teeth like it's another day
The postcards you send from that new city life
Make me throw punches at the edge of the night
I write angry letters but I tear them in two
Cause the truth is I'm broken missing you

I miss you like crazy in this empty town
The walls keep repeating the words that went down
Punk rock and heartache on the same damn track
I want you back now and I ain't looking back

So here's to the distance and the shit that it brings
I'll keep the light on till you spread your wings

songs.subject: trap / coming back home
songs.lyrics:
Back home where the porch light still flickers slow
Gray beard in the mirror from the life I chose
Trap doors in the kitchen from the deals I ran
Now the grandkids runnin' through the same old plans
Broke down Cadillac sittin' in the drive
Young ones askin' questions 'bout how I stayed alive
Came back with nothin' but a pocket full of scars
Neighbors still whisperin' 'bout them prison bars
Back home, back home, streets whisper my name
Back home, back home, nothin' left the same
Back home, back home, where the roots pull tight

I pour another coffee and remember what I never said

songs.subject: alt rock / an old photograph
songs.lyrics:
Behind the register taped above the well the photo curls at the edge
Two kids with paper crowns and grins that never learned to fake
The jukebox plays the same three chords that echo through the smoke
I pour another round and wonder where the laughter went

Holding onto moments that the years refuse to name

The regulars all ask me why I never take it down
I shrug and slide the whiskey like the answer's in the glass
That photo saw the first kiss and the last fight in the rain
Now it's faded at the corners but it still remembers names
I lock the door at midnight with the picture in my hand
Another night of serving ghosts that only I understand

Broke but happy that's the way we roll
Laughin at the rain with a pocket full of soul
No riches here just the love we found

It's these simple moments that never get old

Raising these two kids on what's left of me

I hold to the promise that the storm will pass

Count every blessing in the words He said
From the first light of dawn to the last star at night
Faith keeps the fire when the money's tight

He's the light in the dark when the rent is due
The strength in my hands when the work ain't through

The world outside keeps moving without me
But I'm still here holding what time won't file

songs.subject: gospel / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
I pour the last whiskey as the midnight bell rings out
Another traveler leaves with a heavy heart and doubt
The road stretches long under a sky of endless grace
Lord I'm serving strangers but it's You who sets the pace
We chase the dawn together though the miles may tear us down
Your light keeps shining brighter than this worn out neon crown
On this long road trip through the shadows and the rain
Jesus hold the wheel and ease the ache and pain
Hallelujah brothers keep the faith and drive
We'll meet again in glory when these weary miles arrive
The bottles line the shelf like memories of the past
Folks come in for comfort but the road calls loud and fast
I wipe the counter clean and whisper prayers for those who roam
May Your mercy guide them safely to their heavenly home
Some nights I wonder if I'll ever hit that open highway
But Your word is my compass till the breaking of the day
On this long road trip through the shadows and the rain
Jesus hold the wheel and ease the ache and pain
Hallelujah brothers keep the faith and drive
We'll meet again in glory when these weary miles arrive
The jukebox plays old hymns as the last one heads for home
I lock the door behind me and the spirit starts to roam
With every mile I travel Your promise lights the way
From bartender to believer on this gospel highway
On this long road trip through the shadows and the rain
Jesus hold the wheel and ease the ache and pain
Hallelujah brothers keep the faith and drive
We'll meet again in glory when these weary miles arrive

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a road you keep driving
songs.lyrics:
The tires hum the same old tune on asphalt cracked and gray
I pass the fields where we once played in youth that slipped away
Each curve reveals another year I swore I'd leave behind
Yet here I am still gripping tight the wheel of stubborn mind
The rearview shows a face that's lined with stories left untold
But something pulls me forward on this road that won't grow old
I keep on driving though the signs all point me home
Through the dust and fading light I never feel alone
Memories ride shotgun whispering what might have been
But the engine sings its steady song again and again

songs.subject: lo-fi / starting over
songs.lyrics:

Coffee gone cold but the window stays cracked

Starting over feels like static on the line
Fuzzy and soft but the signal's still mine
Starting over in the hum of the fridge
Learning the quiet like a brand new bridge
Shades half drawn on a borrowed sunrise
Learning to breathe without the old lies
Footsteps echo in a room that's too bare
But the air tastes different when nobody's there
Starting over feels like static on the line
Fuzzy and soft but the signal's still mine
Starting over in the hum of the fridge
Learning the quiet like a brand new bridge
Records spin slow in a lo-fi repeat
Every scratch a story that refuses to keep
I close my eyes and the world starts to spin
Into something softer I can live in

Learning the quiet like a brand new bridge

songs.subject: punk / a first apartment
songs.lyrics:
Four thin walls and a lock that don't stick
Boot prints on the floor from the last guy quick

First place that's mine after what I've been through
Ripped the tags off a mattress on the floor
No more racks no more orders no more war

Freedom tastes like cheap beer and a new keyhole

This apartment's mine even if the paint peels nice

Still better than letters from a faraway grave

songs.subject: americana / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
Woke up with your ghost in the kitchen light
Coffee gone cold and the radio sighs
Trucks roll by on the old two-lane blacktop
Leaving me here with what we ain't got
Dust on the Bible and holes in my boots
Every mile marker cuts like the truth
Oh heartbreak highway don't lead me home
Your love's just a shadow that won't leave me alone
I chase that red sun till it sinks in the ground
But the night always finds me with your name in my sound
Wheels keep turning on this endless road
Your picture in the mirror starts to fade and erode
Bars on the corner where we danced that July
Now the jukebox plays songs that make grown men cry
I pour out the bottle for all that we lost
Counting the hours and the lines that it cost
Oh heartbreak highway don't lead me home
Your love's just a shadow that won't leave me alone
I chase that red sun till it sinks in the ground
But the night always finds me with your name in my sound
Crossed the state line with the radio low
Praying the static might drown out your ghost
But every town sign reads the same old refrain
Another goodbye on this lonesome terrain

songs.lyrics:
Windows down on the blacktop stretch
Wind in my face washing out the rest
Dog tags clinking like a slow drum beat

Trying to forget what the orders say back
Rollin with the night air cool on my skin
Thinking of home and the shape you're in
Driving with the windows down
Freedom feels like it's almost found
But the miles between us stretch so wide
Soldier heart still holding you inside
Baseball cap low and the stars coming out
Memories flashing like the lines on the route
Left you at the gate with that last long kiss
Now the engine hums what I still miss
Open highway and the smell of rain
Pushing the pedal to forget the pain
Driving with the windows down
Freedom feels like it's almost found
But the miles between us stretch so wide
Soldier heart still holding you inside
Sunrise coming on the eastern line
One more day till I'm calling you mine

songs.subject: country / a long-distance relationship
songs.lyrics:
Poured another round for the lonely tonight
Wiping down the bar under the neon light
Your last call came through at a quarter to two
Voice crackin static but the words still true
Told me the job's got you tied to that town
And the miles keep adding while I'm here pourin rounds
I nod at the regulars and force a smile
Counting the tips but I'm counting the miles
Long distance love in a shot glass high
You on the highway and me on the fly
We toast to tomorrow that never arrives
Tending this bar while our hearts survive
Last customer gone and the jukebox slow
I lock up the till and let the memories flow
Your picture taped up by the register keys
Smiling like summer through the winter freeze
I mix one for myself when the shift is done
Wondering how long till the next call comes
Long distance love in a shot glass high
You on the highway and me on the fly
We toast to tomorrow that never arrives
Tending this bar while our hearts survive
Dawn through the blinds and another day starts
Wiping the counters with a heavier heart

songs.subject: lo-fi / summer ending
songs.lyrics:
August heat still lingers on the page
Fan spinning slow in this quiet space
Ice melting in the glass by my side
Every note I write feels like a goodbye
Crickets hum low like they know what's near
The last of the light slipping into the year
Diary open and the ink runs thin
Summer's fading and I'm letting it in
Windows cracked to the cooling breeze
Memories scattered like falling leaves
Nothing stays golden forever it seems
Just these lines and the end of a dream
Sweat on the sheets from the long hot nights
Now the mornings arrive with softer light
I press the flowers between these lines
Hoping the color might survive the time
Radio low and the static feels right
Like the season closing out the night
Windows cracked to the cooling breeze
Memories scattered like falling leaves
Nothing stays golden forever it seems
Just these lines and the end of a dream

songs.subject: trap / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
Last bell's ringing but my boots hit the ground

I ain't going home till the mission is blown

songs.subject: alt rock / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
We met in the spring when the world felt brand new
Now the autumn leaves fall on the life we outgrew
Your voice in the wind still cuts straight through
Heartbreak at my age leaves nothing to do
I sit on the porch with the photos so worn
Remembering the nights when our laughter was born
The years took their toll and the fire is gone
An old man alone at the break of the dawn

You walked away slow but the leaving still stings
In the mirror I see all the gray that it brings
We promised forever with our old wedding rings
Now the silence is louder than any song sings
Heartbreak don't fade when you're counting the days
It settles like dust on the paths that we made
I call out your name in these empty hallways
An old man still hoping for one final phase

The garden we planted has long gone to seed
Your chair at the table is empty indeed
I talk to the wind like it's you that I need
Heartbreak from the past is a wound that won't bleed
Out here in the twilight with nothing but time
The memories linger like an old favorite rhyme
You found someone new in your own springtime prime
I'm left with the echoes of our love in decline

songs.subject: emo / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
Bottles in the trash and the kids are asleep
I'm fighting the urge that still runs so deep
Sober feels heavy like a promise I keep
For their little faces I won't let it creep
Tears on the counter where the old life would pour
An empty glass mocking from the days before
Single mom rising from the wreckage on the floor
Getting clean for them opens every door

No more blurry mornings waking up in shame
I'm learning to stand without playing the game
The shakes and the cravings still call out my name
But their tiny hands pull me back from the flame
Sober is lonely in this quiet midnight
I journal the battles and the wrongs I made right
Single mother armor shining in the light
Getting clean for them gives me strength to fight

The bills stack higher and the past pulls me down
Yet their laughter echoes turning it around
I choose every morning not to wear the crown
Of the bottle that once made me drown
Sober ain't easy but it's all that I own
For the future they're building I'm no longer alone
Single mom warrior carving out a home
Getting clean for them is the seed that I've sown

Diary pages are flying like embers into the flame

Running away is the only truth under these skies

Running from shadows that I painted in every room

The jukebox plays our song but it brings only pain

Taps keep flowing but the laughter's gone
I serve up shots while the memories drone on

songs.subject: americana / heartbreak
songs.lyrics:
Porch swing creaks in the evening breeze blowing
Your photograph faded in my calloused hand
We danced in the kitchen to records from our youth
Now the record spins lonely and I barely can stand
Fields we once walked stretch empty and silent
The tractor sits rusting where we parked it that spring
Your laugh used to echo across these old acres

Time moves like river but the heart stays broken
Love don't come back when it packs up and leaves
I sit here and wonder what I could have done different
With my hat in my hands and these memories to grieve

Neighbors still wave when they pass by the driveway
They tip their hats low like they know what I've lost
I nod and I smile but the smile don't reach deeper
Than the lines on my face from the years that we've crossed
The barn needs repairing but my back won't allow it
The supper table's set for a meal I can't eat
Your coat still hangs by the door like a promise
That someday you'll walk back but I know you're not coming this week

Time moves like river but the heart stays broken
Love don't come back when it packs up and leaves
I sit here and wonder what I could have done different
With my hat in my hands and these memories to grieve

songs.lyrics:

Hiding all the worry behind goodnight lies
Working this dead end just to get by

Holding it together with a mother's grace
Hoping tomorrow finds a better place

One more sunrise on this weary road

songs.subject: americana / a road you keep driving
songs.lyrics:
Headlights cutting through the dark highway line
Engine humming low like an old friend of mine
Windows down the wind's calling my name again
Chasing faded signs that never seem to end
White lines blur as the miles roll on by

This road I keep driving never lets me stay
Dust and memories fading in the rearview gray
Sun comes up then it falls back down
Still I'm rolling through another nameless town
Gas station coffee and a radio song
Stories in the static where I don't belong
Tires wear thin on the asphalt's endless bend
Hoping one day this journey finds an end
This road I keep driving never lets me stay
Dust and memories fading in the rearview gray
Sun comes up then it falls back down
Still I'm rolling through another nameless town
Night falls heavy but the wheels turn slow
Carrying the load of the life I know
One more mile marker in the pouring rain
This road keeps calling and I can't complain

songs.subject: reggae / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:

Dance alone in the kitchen feel the bass line in my chest

Morning comes too early and the orders start again
But tonight the kitchen's mine and I'm dancing like a man
Who lost too many brothers but can still feel the sun
Through the speakers in the corner where the real war is won

songs.subject: dream pop / saying goodbye at an airport
songs.lyrics:
Wheels are turning on the runway in the haze of early light
I watch you walking through the gate and nothing feels quite right
My hands are shaking on the page where I write it all tonight
Like the clouds are swallowing the words I couldn't say
You turned once more before the doors and everything went gray
Diary pages filling with the things we never play
Over and over in my mind the moment slips away

Waving through the glass until my arm falls down
Airport lights are fading like a half-remembered sound
Waving through the glass as the plane begins to rise
Writing in the diary with tears behind my eyes

The terminal is crowded but I'm standing all alone
Ticket in my pocket for a flight I won't take home
Memories like vapor trails across the painted dome
You promised you'd remember but the distance makes it thin
I trace your outline on the window with my finger on the skin
Diary spilling secrets that the engines can't begin
To carry through the sky where every ending starts again

Waving through the glass until my arm falls down
Airport lights are fading like a half-remembered sound
Waving through the glass as the plane begins to rise
Writing in the diary with tears behind my eyes

Now the night is settling and the pages blur with rain
I close the book and wonder if I'll see you here again

songs.lyrics:

You changed on me my friend and the light is burning out

songs.subject: americana / getting sober
songs.lyrics:
I woke up in the backseat of my friend's old truck
Empty bottles rolling like they knew I was stuck
Momma called again but I let it ring out
Prom night came and went and I just stayed on the couch
High school hallways smelling like cheap perfume and lies
I was chasing that buzz just to quiet the noise inside
But last weekend at the river I poured it all in the mud
Said I'm done pretending this is who I'm made of

I'm walking away from the fire I used to chase
Learning how to face tomorrow with a clear head on my face
It ain't pretty and it hurts like hell some nights
But I'm getting sober for the kid I used to be in the lights

Coach pulled me aside said my numbers been slipping
I told him the truth and he just stood there listening
No more fake IDs and no more skipping class
I'm trading late nights for sunrise and second chances
My little brother asked why I ain't coming out no more
I said I'm trying to be someone he can look up for
The parties still calling but I'm learning to say no
One day at a time and that's all I know

I'm walking away from the fire I used to chase
Learning how to face tomorrow with a clear head on my face
It ain't pretty and it hurts like hell some nights
But I'm getting sober for the kid I used to be in the lights

songs.subject: americana / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
Boot prints in the sand and your last letter in my hand
You left for the city while I shipped out to this land
The sky here burns orange like the porch light you used to leave
I stare at the horizon wishing you were still beside me
Base camp radio plays songs we used to sing

You said you'd wait forever but the miles just kept on growing
Now I'm counting down the days while my heart keeps on roaming

I miss the way you waved goodbye on that old dirt road
Now I'm halfway around the world and the silence feels too cold

Nights are long and quiet when the mission lights go down
I trace your name in the dust like it could turn this all around
You moved away for reasons that I swore I understood
But every empty bunk reminds me what we said we would
Home feels like a dream now but I keep it in my chest
Praying when I make it back you'll still be there like you promised

I miss the way you waved goodbye on that old dirt road
