songs.subject: lo-fi / growing up in a small town
songs.lyrics:
I pour the whiskey slow under neon lights that flicker
Same faces every night in this town that time forgot
We talk about the old mill and the kids who never leave
I wipe the bar and smile at stories from the parking lot
The river still runs lazy past the high school football field
Where we all dreamed of cities but came back when it got real
Empty bottles line the shelves like years we can't get back
I nod along and listen as the jukebox plays the past

Small town nights they linger on the edges of my mind
Pour another round and let the memories unwind
Growing up and staying put it's all the same old song
In this bar I learned the words to where I still belong

The corner store still sells the same old brand of cigarettes
I serve the ones who stayed and hear about the ones who left
Tractors rumble down the main street when the harvest comes
I close up late and walk home past the faded welcome sign
High school sweethearts now have kids who ask me for a coke
The same old jokes get told again and laughter fills the smoke
I dream of somewhere bigger but the keys are in my hand
This place it holds me gentle like a worn out rubber band

Small town nights they linger on the edges of my mind
Pour another round and let the memories unwind
Growing up and staying put it's all the same old song
In this bar I learned the words to where I still belong

songs.subject: americana / being broke but happy
songs.lyrics:
My boots are caked in desert sand my wallet's paper thin
I send half my pay back home and still I grin
A letter from my girl says the truck needs fixing bad
I laugh and tell my buddies 'bout the good we never had
Beans and rice for dinner but the stars are shining bright
We trade old stories 'round the fire and it feels just right
No money for the weekend but the sunset's free for all
I hum a little tune and watch the evening fall

Broke and happy on this winding road
Heart still beating strong through the heavy load
No silver in my pocket but the sky is gold
Broke and happy living young and old

The barracks hold our laughter when the letters don't arrive
We count our blessings in the dust and keep the hope alive
A guitar with two strings makes a song that feels complete
We pass around a photo of the kids back on the street
No fancy clothes or dinners but the coffee's always hot
I thank the Lord for simple things that others might have forgot
When the mission's over and the miles stretch out ahead
I carry all the love I need inside my weary head

No silver in my pocket but the sky is gold
Broke and happy living young and old

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
I got the call while stationed overseas the line was full of rain
Mama said to hold on tight but I could feel the pain
The flag they folded at the church still hangs inside my mind
I pour out stories to the wind of the strength I left behind
Her letters in my duffel bag are worn from reading twice
She taught me how to stand up straight and love without a price
Now every step I take feels like I'm walking back to home
The uniform is heavy but her voice is never gone

I raise a glass to heaven in a bar across the sea
Whiskey burns like memories of who you used to be
The war goes on without you but your song still guides me through
Losing you has changed the man I swore I'd always be

The chaplain offered comfort but the ache is mine to keep
I see your face in every crowd and hear you in my sleep
The boys don't ask me questions when I stare off at the wall
They know the weight of family and the silence after all
I write these lines by flashlight in a tent beneath the stars
Hoping you can hear them from wherever angels are
The service is tomorrow and I'll stand there straight and tall
Carrying your lessons through the battles yet to call

I raise a glass to heaven in a bar across the sea
Whiskey burns like memories of who you used to be
The war goes on without you but your song still guides me through
Losing you has changed the man I swore I'd always be

Your voice cuts through the static from a city far away
I mix a drink and listen to the miles between our words

The nights get lonely but your laugh still echoes in my head
I send a voice note saying all the words I never said
Trap songs play on repeat about the ones who fight for love
I count the hours till your next call rises up above

songs.subject: country / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
The phone rang past the midnight hour, whiskey burning in my glass
Your voice came through like a ghost from the past, sayin' you miss what we had
I heard the crickets singin' outside my old truck parked in the drive
And every word you whispered pulled me back to the nights we were alive
You said the road got lonely without my arms to hold you tight
But I remember the fights that lit up like fireworks in the night
Now the moon's hangin' low and the static's cracklin' on the line
I'm wonderin' if this call means you're comin' home or just killin' time

Baby call me in the dark when the world's gone quiet
Tell me all the things we lost in the glow of that dashboard light
Midnight phone call, heart still achin' for the truth
Even if it breaks me again I'm reachin' out for you

I lit another cigarette and listened to your shaky breath
You talked about the regrets that followed every step you left
The porch light flickered like our love that burned too fast too bright
And I wondered if you'd hang up before I could ask you to stay the night
Tractors hummin' in the distance, old radio playin' our song
Your tears sounded like rain on the tin roof where we used to belong
I wanted to drive through the dark but my boots stayed planted here
Caught between the past and the silence that followed your words so clear

Baby call me in the dark when the world's gone quiet
Tell me all the things we lost in the glow of that dashboard light
Midnight phone call, heart still achin' for the truth
Even if it breaks me again I'm reachin' out for you

The line went dead like a highway at dawn, leavin' me alone with the ache
But that midnight ring still echoes every time the lonely wind shakes

songs.lyrics:
Rolled into this new town with a duffel and a dream in my grip
Bartender nights servin' shots while the city lights start to flip
Used to know every face back home now it's strangers at the bar
Mixing drinks for the brokenhearted wonderin' who they really are
Pour another round for the guy who lost his job last week
Listen to his story while the bass from the speakers leak
New city got me hustlin' from open to close of the spot
Learnin' names and numbers but I'm still connectin' the dots
From the corner to the avenue I'm pourin' out the truth in a glass
Tryna build a life from nothin' watchin' all the faces pass

New city rhythm got me spinnin' like a record on repeat
Bartender mixin' dreams with the hustle on these streets
Every pour is a chance every tip is a step ahead
Movin' on from yesterday leavin' old ghosts for dead

Late shifts and early mornings coffee black to keep me straight
Watchin' the skyline grow on me like it's sealin' my fate
Old friends call but the signal fades in the urban roar
I'm stackin' these hours for a future behind this wooden door
From the dive bar crowd to the suits at the after-work rush
Every story's a verse and I'm the one who makes it hush
New city grind but I'm findin' my flow in the mix
Slingin' liquor and wisdom hopin' the rhythm sticks

New city rhythm got me spinnin' like a record on repeat
Bartender mixin' dreams with the hustle on these streets
Every pour is a chance every tip is a step ahead
Movin' on from yesterday leavin' old ghosts for dead

songs.subject: trap / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
Last bell rang and the kids rushed out like freedom in the air
Old man on the porch rockin' slow watchin' without a care
Backpacks flyin' laughter echoin' down the empty hall
Reminds me of my own last day when the world felt so tall
Now my hands shake more than they used to chalk dust long gone
Watchin' youth chase dreams while I hum an old forgotten song
Summer stretchin' out ahead for them no more books or rules
I'm sippin' on sweet tea thinkin' back on my own last schools

Trap beats in my memory from when I ran these same streets wild
Last day of school feelin' like the start of somethin' styled
Old eyes see the same fire in these young ones runnin' free
Last day of school and it all comes back to me

Grandsons textin' about parties plans for the night to come
I'm rememberin' my own escapes when the school year was done
No more tests no more teachers just the open road ahead
But time has a way of slowin' you down till the fire starts to spread
Porch swing creakin' like the old days when I was their age too
Last day of school got me reflectin' on the paths I knew
Kids with futures bright as neon in the trap of life
Old man smilin' quiet knowin' every end brings a new strife

Trap beats in my memory from when I ran these same streets wild
Last day of school feelin' like the start of somethin' styled
Old eyes see the same fire in these young ones runnin' free

songs.subject: dream pop / moving to a new city
songs.lyrics:
In the quiet of my worn-out room I fold the years away
Boxes filled with photographs that time has turned to gray
The train pulls out at dawn and I watch the fields go by
An old man chasing neon dreams beneath an endless sky
The city hums like distant waves against my tired bones
I chase the lights through crowded streets that never sleep alone
Memories of her soft laugh drift like autumn leaves
In this new world I wander lost among the young and free

And the city calls me forward though my steps are slow and frail
In the haze of midnight colors I can still prevail
Dreams unfold like paper boats on rivers made of sound
An old heart learns to wander where the lost are found

Elevators rise like prayers through towers made of glass
I see my face reflected in the strangers as they pass
Coffee shops and corner bars hold stories I don’t know
Yet every window glows with lives that flicker like a show
I walk until the dawn arrives and paints the rooftops gold
Feeling young inside a body that has quietly grown old
The skyline sways in gentle waves as traffic starts to sing
An old man finds his second chance in everything

And the city calls me forward though my steps are slow and frail
In the haze of midnight colors I can still prevail
Dreams unfold like paper boats on rivers made of sound
An old heart learns to wander where the lost are found

songs.subject: soul / losing a parent
songs.lyrics:
The phone call came too early and the words fell like rain
Now the house feels empty and the silence stays the same
I reach for your old sweater hanging by the door
But your voice has gone forever and I can’t call anymore

You taught me how to stand when the world was falling down
You held my hand through every storm in this little town
Now I walk these rooms alone with your picture in my hand
Trying to remember how it felt to understand

Mama I still hear you when the wind moves through the trees
Your laughter in the kitchen and your prayers upon the breeze
I’m learning how to carry on though my heart is torn in two
Every song I sing is missing the part that came from you

The kitchen clock keeps ticking but the meals have lost their taste
I set a place for you each night though I know you’ve been erased
Your garden grows without you now the flowers still bloom bright
But I water them with tears that fall like prayers into the night

Mama I still hear you when the wind moves through the trees
Your laughter in the kitchen and your prayers upon the breeze
I’m learning how to carry on though my heart is torn in two
Every song I sing is missing the part that came from you

songs.subject: rock / a new baby
songs.lyrics:
The world just flipped around when the doctor said it’s time
Now the crib waits empty and my hands are shaking fine
I used to chase the night with nothing left to lose
Now I’m counting tiny fingers and learning brand new rules

Rock me through the thunder when the little lungs cry out
I’ll stand here in the dark and never walk away from doubt
This tiny life is screaming and it’s got me by the throat
I’m a new man now and I’ll never be the same again

Diapers on the floor and coffee cold at 4 a.m.
I trade my leather jacket for a bottle and a hymn
The boys still call me out but I just smile and wave
Because this little heartbeat owns the soul I used to save

Rock me through the thunder when the little lungs cry out
I’ll stand here in the dark and never walk away from doubt
This tiny life is screaming and it’s got me by the throat
I’m a new man now and I’ll never be the same again

I used to think that freedom meant the open road ahead
Now freedom is the sound of breathing in a tiny bed
The nights are long and louder than the music ever was
But I’d burn the whole damn stage down just to hear that laugh again

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / a city at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The streetlights hum like tired ghosts on avenues gone still
My footsteps echo off the glass of shops locked tight until
The city breathes in empty sighs beneath a yellow moon
And every corner holds a secret whispered far too soon

I walk past windows glowing blue with lives I’ll never meet
A couple laughing on a stoop while taxis crawl the street
The river glints like broken glass and carries dreams away
At 3 a.m. the whole world pauses in a quiet gray

And I keep moving through the dark where no one knows my name
Chasing silence like an old familiar flame

The bars have closed their heavy doors and left the chairs upside
A saxophone somewhere moans about the love that died
My coat is thin against the wind that slips between the towers
I count the blocks until the dawn can bring back all the hours

And I keep moving through the dark where no one knows my name
Chasing silence like an old familiar flame

A single bus rolls by with one light on inside
I watch it vanish down the block and feel the city slide
Into the gray that comes before the morning starts to rise
At 3 a.m. I’m still the only witness to the skies

songs.subject: bluegrass / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Wheels keep turning on this old gray road
My hands are steady but my back's feeling the load
Left that farmhouse at the break of dawn
Heading west where the rivers carry on
The radio plays tunes from years gone by
As the miles slip under this truck of mine
Thinking of the woman who once rode along
Now it's just me and these memories strong
Rolling down the highway under endless skies
Old man chasing sunsets with weary eyes
Every town I pass brings a story to my mind
But the road keeps calling and I'm leaving them behind
Stopped for coffee at a diner by the tracks
Talked to a stranger about the years that don't come back
He said the world's changed but the asphalt stays the same
I nodded slow and headed back into the rain
The mountains rise up like they did in sixty three
When I first drove this rig with a young man's dream
Now the aches remind me that the end is near
Still I push the pedal like I've got nothing to fear
Rolling down the highway under endless skies
Old man chasing sunsets with weary eyes
Every town I pass brings a story to my mind
But the road keeps calling and I'm leaving them behind

songs.subject: country / missing someone who moved away
songs.lyrics:
The fields outside my window look the same
But your chair at the table feels empty all the same
You packed your bags for that city far away
Left me here with the bills and the bills to pay
I still dial your number when the nights get long
Hear your voice in the wind singing our old song
Miss the way you'd laugh at my silly old jokes
Now the silence is louder than the coyotes' howls
Every letter that you sent I read again and again
Hoping one day you'll come walking back in
But the train tracks are quiet and the seasons turn slow
I'm still here missing you wherever you go
The porch light burns bright every single night
Waiting for your shadow in the pale moonlight
Your sweater still hangs on the hook by the door
Carries the scent of the life we had before
Every letter that you sent I read again and again
Hoping one day you'll come walking back in
But the train tracks are quiet and the seasons turn slow
I'm still here missing you wherever you go

songs.subject: alt rock / a breakup text

Your message came through like a knife in the dark
Said we were done no more room for this spark
I read it three times couldn't catch my breath
You were always the fire I was fighting for
Now the screen is blank but the hurt remains
I'm begging the silence to say it's all a game
We had plans for forever or so I believed
One line on a phone and my whole world grieved
Tell me it's not over don't leave me here alone
This love still burns bright like it did back home
I can fix what broke just give me the chance
Don't end it with letters don't kill our romance
The pictures on my wall still show your smile
I scroll through the past and it hurts for a while
You said you needed space but this feels like goodbye
I'm stuck in the loop asking myself why
Tell me it's not over don't leave me here alone
This love still burns bright like it did back home
I can fix what broke just give me the chance

songs.subject: americana / dancing alone in the kitchen
songs.lyrics:
The dishes are stacked and the radio's low
Bare feet on linoleum moving real slow
Baby's asleep in the room down the hall
I sway with the silence and lean on the wall
Moon through the curtains paints silver on chrome
I close my eyes and I'm not all alone

Dance with me baby just one more time
Even when nobody's holding this line
Kitchen light fading but the song keeps on
Single mom spinning till the heartache is gone

Old faded photos are taped to the fridge
Laughing together on some long ago bridge
Now it's just me and the clock on the stove
Turning the heartache into something like hope
Bare cabinets echo the beat in my chest
I keep on dancing though nobody's left

Dance with me baby just one more time
Even when nobody's holding this line
Kitchen light fading but the song keeps on
Single mom spinning till the heartache is gone

Morning will come with its crayons and calls

songs.subject: bluegrass / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:

Windows down and the radio fine
Me and my buddy with a map that's torn
Headin north where the mountains are born

But the freedom's callin and the world's all mine
Gas station coffee and a pack of smokes
Telling jokes till the morning light pokes

Rollin down the highway singin loud
Every mile feels like a brand new crowd
Long road trip with the future ahead
Teenage hearts racin wild and unfed
Rollin down the highway under the moon
Windows fogged with the radio tune
We got nothin but time and a tank of dreams
Chasin the sunrise down these winding streams

Stopped in a diner for a slice of pie
Talked to a waitress with a wink in her eye
Said she's been waitin on a ride just like ours
To somewhere bigger under bigger stars
We laughed till it hurt then hit the gas
Countin the exits as the miles passed
No plans no rules just the open road
Feelin alive in this heavy load

songs.subject: pop / a midnight phone call
songs.lyrics:
It's two in the morning and the screen lights up blue
Your name in the dark cuts right straight through
I pick up the phone even though I know it's wrong
You said we were done but you're calling me now
Midnight phone call with your voice breaking down
Telling me sorry like it fixes the hurt
I’m staring at the ceiling while my chest caves in
You left me in pieces and you’re dialing again

Why you calling at midnight after letting me go
Tears on the line but I already know
You broke what we had now you’re reaching for more
Midnight phone call I can’t take it no more

songs.subject: trap / a long road trip
songs.lyrics:
Rolling out the city with the radio low
Bottles in the back from the shift I just closed
Highway lines glowing under tired eyes
Pouring drinks all night now the open road flies
Stacking up the miles while the sun starts to rise
Memories of bar fights and the same old lies
Tip jar empty but my mind stays full
Every exit sign looks like a new kind of pull

Long road calling got the pedal down slow
Trap beats bumping while the dashboard glows
From the bar to the highway chasing what I owe
Long road calling every mile I know

Counting up the hours since the last drink was poured
Neon lights fading in the rearview mirror
Friends I left behind all chasing their own score
Windows down the wind feels like an open door
Gas tank humming and the thoughts start to swarm
Bartender dreams in the eye of the storm
City lights behind me turning into dust
Freedom in the distance but it’s never quite enough

Long road calling got the pedal down slow
Trap beats bumping while the dashboard glows
From the bar to the highway chasing what I owe
Long road calling every mile I know

songs.subject: singer-songwriter / the last day of school
songs.lyrics:
Lockers slam shut for the final time today
Backpacks heavy with the years we gave away
Hallways echo with the laughter fading slow
We’re stepping out the doors we used to know
Last bell ringing like a promise in the air
Friends waving goodbye from the parking lot glare
Summer stretches endless but the past pulls tight
We’re trading these four walls for whatever’s right

We’re leaving now the lessons stay behind
Chasing open skies with uncertain minds
Last day of school and the future feels so wide
We’re leaving now with nothing left to hide

Yearbooks signed with words we hope we keep
Dreams we wrote down in the notebooks deep
Teachers smiling like they know we’ll be okay
We’re walking out together at the end of May
Pictures on the phone of every class we took
Prom nights and failures in the same old book
The field behind the gym where we used to run
Now it’s just a memory under setting sun

We’re leaving now the lessons stay behind
Chasing open skies with uncertain minds
Last day of school and the future feels so wide
We’re leaving now with nothing left to hide

songs.subject: alt rock / your hometown
songs.lyrics:
Main street still cracks under boots that used to play
The factory whistle’s silent since the last shift went away
I walk these blocks again with dust on my uniform
Hometown smells the same like rain and diesel warm
The corner bar still stands where we swore we’d never leave
Now I’m back between the houses trying hard to breathe
Schoolyard empty echoes every fight we used to win
Soldier boots on pavement feel like strangers in my skin

Concrete rivers running through the place I left for war
Every broken window holds the kid I was before
Hometown lights are fading but they still know my name
Concrete rivers pulling me back through the flame

The river bridge we jumped from still carries the same wind
Names carved in the railing where our old world began
I see my mother’s porch light cutting through the night
But the war still sits behind me like a shadow out of sight
Trains rattle past the tracks that used to take me far
Now they bring me home to faces marked by every scar
This town keeps breathing even when the young ones disappear
I’m standing on the corner with the weight of every year

Concrete rivers running through the place I left for war
Every broken window holds the kid I was before
Hometown lights are fading but they still know my name
Concrete rivers pulling me back through the flame
